* * *
* * *
### The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication
### Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism
[Support What We Do](https://substack.com/redirect/d6ce1241-ea2d-4d4f-92ca-7063540e594e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/20e4ad93-3013-4b45-8890-614b293d369a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5e603952-e050-45fd-a157-3175b8f5eb67?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
In Michigan, blueprints for a colossal data center lie dormant, their promise of powering the next era of artificial intelligence now shrouded in uncertainty.
Oracle, the software behemoth once synonymous with steadfast enterprise solutions, had trumpeted this $10 billion project as a cornerstone of its AI ambitions--a sprawling facility designed to house the computational muscle for generative AI models and cloud services.
Yet, on a mid-December day in 2025, news broke that Blue Owl Capital, a key private lender, had abruptly withdrawn from financing discussions, sending Oracle's shares into a tailspin.
This wasn't just a corporate hiccup; it was a stark reminder of how the AI hype machine, fueled by press releases and lofty projections, is colliding with the unforgiving realities of debt, future obsolescence, and market skepticism.
As investors fled, Oracle's stock plummeted more than 5% in a single session down to $177, capping an 18% monthly decline that has contributed to a nearly 50% tumble from their September high of $345.72. What had began as a narrative of boundless innovation now looks like anything but.
Oracle's descent underscores a broader tension in the tech landscape, where aggressive expansion meets financial fragility. The company's pivot to AI, **[heralded by former CEO Safra Catz](https://substack.com/redirect/4b31e3a2-7925-4d45-8f2e-774eccc550cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in earnings calls as a "once-in-a-generation opportunity," involved a frenzy of deal-making that scaled up its data center footprint at breakneck speed. Just months earlier, Oracle had inked partnerships for **[similar ventures in Texas and New Mexico,](https://substack.com/redirect/de8ab0c7-631e-4b28-af2a-9c8c278baad5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** collaborating with Blue Owl on facilities valued in the billions.
The Michigan project alone aimed to deliver nearly one gigawatt of power capacity, enough to rival small nuclear plants, to support hyperscale AI training. These deals were pitched as transformative, with Oracle projecting $100 billion in annual cloud revenue by the end of the decade. But the scale masked underlying vulnerabilities: unlike peers such as Microsoft or Amazon, Oracle lacked the internal cash flows to self-fund such endeavors, relying instead on a web of external financing that now appears increasingly shaky.
At the heart of Oracle's strategy lay **[innovative but risky financing mechanisms,](https://substack.com/redirect/7441fdd7-bc6c-4a38-b7c7-194fec8070b0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/7441fdd7-bc6c-4a38-b7c7-194fec8070b0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)blending traditional debt with off-balance-sheet leases so as to sidestep immediate capital outlays. By November 2025, the company's lease commitments had **[ballooned to $248 billion,](https://substack.com/redirect/d74c1310-efad-4510-b7be-d33ec1b17504?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** a nearly 150% increase from prior levels, primarily tied to long-term data center and cloud capacity agreements spanning 15 to 19 years.
According to Oracle's quarterly SEC filing, these obligations were structured to accommodate surging AI demand, allowing the firm to expand **[without diluting equity or tapping reserves.](https://substack.com/redirect/2a2347e2-407b-4f1e-bda6-595412348aa6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** This approach mirrored a broader industry trend, where tech giants outsource infrastructure risks to lenders and developers. Oracle supplemented this with bond issuances and partnerships, maintaining a BBB investment-grade rating on its debt. Yet, critics argue this model creates hidden leverage: total debt, including short- and long-term obligations, exceeded $111 billion by August, up sharply[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/d216ccb4-c271-43a7-a3f4-bfced850223a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[from the previous year.](https://substack.com/redirect/d216ccb4-c271-43a7-a3f4-bfced850223a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** When Blue Owl balked at the Michigan deal--**despite** successful prior collaborations--the fragility became evident, exposing Oracle to refinancing risks in an uncertain environment.
Investor reactions were swift and unforgiving, amplifying concerns over Oracle's debt sustainability. Following the Financial Times report on the financing deal collapse, shares dove more than 5%, extending a post-earnings rout where costs outpaced revenue growth **[and cash burn exceeded forecasts.](https://substack.com/redirect/edb2b757-ab91-4738-b981-9cd652b9aa6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/edb2b757-ab91-4738-b981-9cd652b9aa6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Credit markets echoed the alarm: Oracle's five-year credit default swap (CDS) spreads surged to 139 basis points, their highest since the 2009 financial crisis, signaling **[heightened perceptions of a default risk.](https://substack.com/redirect/90a5e35a-acfa-4345-84b3-455e8028b790?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/71d08e21-caea-4870-be52-55b5e2654e30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Analysts at firms like Morningstar noted that these spreads could climb to 150 basis points in short order, and they promptly did.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5b14be0c-c360-4f1e-a8c5-61983ca1f90e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Morningstar also views Oracle as a **["canary in the coal mine"](https://substack.com/redirect/860aaa54-1502-4ab8-9b44-c23a8abf19f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for Big Tech's debt-fueled AI spree. The company's insistence that negotiations with an alternative equity partner were on track did little to quell fears; instead, it highlighted a dependency on fickle external capital. Questions pivoted, where once they were all about growth metrics, now it was about balance sheet health. Oracle's expansion that had been described as a "once-in-a-generation opportunity," now looked a lot more like "building castles in the air."
It should also be noted that while Oracle's decline was spectacular on a sheer percentage basis, the really concerning part of the tumble was the speed at which it happened. Ai if you listen to the chattering press is supposed to be the glittering future, but a major player in a supposedly burgeoning and rapidly growing industry managed to lose 50% of its value in barely 3 months. That sort of whiplash is usually not seen outside of the crypto space and for it to happen to such an established name as Oracle alludes to just how fragile their place in the AI sector can be.
This turmoil also rippled far beyond Oracle, exposing emerging systemic risks in the AI sector where financial interconnections and investor sentiment create a domino effect. On the day of the plunge, the Nasdaq Composite fell sharply, dragged down by AI-linked stocks: GE Vernova tumbled nearly 8%, Broadcom shed 3%, and Nvidia dipped 2%, with ASML, TSMC, and AMD also declining amid[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/0eb64096-5067-46d2-93eb-5a9295542a1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[doubts over AI financing viability.](https://substack.com/redirect/0eb64096-5067-46d2-93eb-5a9295542a1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** The S&P 500's top 10 stocks, which now comprise 41% of its total market cap, amplified the fallout, **[underscoring concentration risks in an AI-driven market.](https://substack.com/redirect/4a118bd7-cd8e-4036-858f-ea4d57bae0a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Bad news for one player--whether it be Oracle's financing snag or broader questions about AI profitability--quickly infects the ecosystem. Hyperscalers like Google and Meta depend on shared suppliers and infrastructure, while investor psychology regarding "AI" tie all their fates together: a retreat in Oracle's cloud ambitions casts shadows on chipmakers and hyperscalers alike.
Compounding these issues is a growing skepticism about the data center model itself, as AI's rapid evolution threatens to render planned massive structural investments obsolete. Billionaire real estate developer Fernando de Leon, who built a $10 billion empire through Leon Capital Group, has emerged as a vocal critic, warning that the sector's hype ignores fundamental flaws. In a recent interview, de Leon pointed to the absence of big exits for multibillion-dollar centers, noting no comparable sales above $4-5 billion **[with which to justify such soaring valuations.](https://substack.com/redirect/62a2b9f1-6dd9-4c27-89e3-3f2127d28544?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
He also questioned why trillion-dollar hyperscalers refuse to own these assets, instead choosing to push the risks onto investors: _**" The AI business is everything for them today, for the large hyperscalers, and so they're saying, 'No, you build it, you finance it.'"**_ De Leon also highlighted tech obsolescence, arguing that AI's self-improving efficiency will devalue the equipment inside, where the true worth lies--not the buildings and campuses.
Moreover, he described long-term leases as **" Swiss cheese,"** riddled with escape clauses that endanger the pension fund-backed capital - hungry for high returns above all else - that have been ploughed in to fund these ventures and could end up holding the bag. As AI models grow more efficient--requiring less compute for greater output--these gargantuan facilities could become white elephants, with billions left to rot in stranded assets.
Oracle's saga along this rocky road over the next few months will illuminate a lot about the AI boom's long term sustainability, as hyped projections meet economic gravity. If even a veteran like Oracle stumbles under their incurred debt's weight, what does that mean for startups chasing similar dreams? Will an industry that is so tightly interconnected heed the warnings, or chase the mirage further into the desert?
This moment could herald a recalibration, where innovation tempers with prudence, or it might presage a broader correction, popping the bubble of unchecked optimism. Whatever the outcome, we have been afforded a taste of the wider market consequences this week. The emerging systemic risk in the AI industry means there will be **A LOT** of collateral damage if something goes wrong.
Oracle is however still a major player, **[the news on Friday](https://substack.com/redirect/83128f64-8181-434a-b076-6bb4d158bf07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that they will be instrumental in the long running TikTok deal negotiations is at least a bright spot on the horizon. Shares are currently up 8% on the year, but the fact that they were up as high as 95% and then lost it all can't be ignored.
Oracle is staying true to its name sake. It is revealing hidden knowledge to us, but it turns out that what we are learning is that true progress demands not just mystical vision, but viable economic foundations.

clean_body: Oracle is staying true to its name sake. It is revealing hidden knowledge to us. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Oracle is staying true to its name sake. It is revealing hidden knowledge to us. | | | Dec 21 | | | | | | | | | | | | The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- In Michigan, blueprints for a colossal data center lie dormant, their promise of powering the next era of artificial intelligence now shrouded in uncertainty. Oracle, the software behemoth once synonymous with steadfast enterprise solutions, had trumpeted this $10 billion project as a cornerstone of its AI ambitions--a sprawling facility designed to house the computational muscle for generative AI models and cloud services. Yet, on a mid-December day in 2025, news broke that Blue Owl Capital, a key private lender, had abruptly withdrawn from financing discussions, sending Oracle's shares into a tailspin. This wasn't just a corporate hiccup; it was a stark reminder of how the AI hype machine, fueled by press releases and lofty projections, is colliding with the unforgiving realities of debt, future obsolescence, and market skepticism. As investors fled, Oracle's stock plummeted more than 5% in a single session down to $177, capping an 18% monthly decline that has contributed to a nearly 50% tumble from their September high of $345.72. What had began as a narrative of boundless innovation now looks like anything but. Oracle's descent underscores a broader tension in the tech landscape, where aggressive expansion meets financial fragility. The company's pivot to AI, in earnings calls as a "once-in-a-generation opportunity," involved a frenzy of deal-making that scaled up its data center footprint at breakneck speed. Just months earlier, Oracle had inked partnerships for collaborating with Blue Owl on facilities valued in the billions. The Michigan project alone aimed to deliver nearly one gigawatt of power capacity, enough to rival small nuclear plants, to support hyperscale AI training. These deals were pitched as transformative, with Oracle projecting $100 billion in annual cloud revenue by the end of the decade. But the scale masked underlying vulnerabilities: unlike peers such as Microsoft or Amazon, Oracle lacked the internal cash flows to self-fund such endeavors, relying instead on a web of external financing that now appears increasingly shaky. At the heart of Oracle's strategy lay traditional debt with off-balance-sheet leases so as to sidestep immediate capital outlays. By November 2025, the company's lease commitments had a nearly 150% increase from prior levels, primarily tied to long-term data center and cloud capacity agreements spanning 15 to 19 years. According to Oracle's quarterly SEC filing, these obligations were structured to accommodate surging AI demand, allowing the firm to expand This approach mirrored a broader industry trend, where tech giants outsource infrastructure risks to lenders and developers. Oracle supplemented this with bond issuances and partnerships, maintaining a BBB investment-grade rating on its debt. Yet, critics argue this model creates hidden leverage: total debt, including short- and long-term obligations, exceeded $111 billion by August, up sharply When Blue Owl balked at the Michigan deal--despite successful prior collaborations--the fragility became evident, exposing Oracle to refinancing risks in an uncertain environment. Investor reactions were swift and unforgiving, amplifying concerns over Oracle's debt sustainability. Following the Financial Times report on the financing deal collapse, shares dove more than 5%, extending a post-earnings rout where costs outpaced revenue growth Credit markets echoed the alarm: Oracle's five-year credit default swap (CDS) spreads surged to 139 basis points, their highest since the 2009 financial crisis, signaling | | ---|---|--- Analysts at firms like Morningstar noted that these spreads could climb to 150 basis points in short order, and they promptly did. | | ---|---|--- Morningstar also views Oracle as a for Big Tech's debt-fueled AI spree. The company's insistence that negotiations with an alternative equity partner were on track did little to quell fears; instead, it highlighted a dependency on fickle external capital. Questions pivoted, where once they were all about growth metrics, now it was about balance sheet health. Oracle's expansion that had been described as a "once-in-a-generation opportunity," now looked a lot more like "building castles in the air." It should also be noted that while Oracle's decline was spectacular on a sheer percentage basis, the really concerning part of the tumble was the speed at which it happened. Ai if you listen to the chattering press is supposed to be the glittering future, but a major player in a supposedly burgeoning and rapidly growing industry managed to lose 50% of its value in barely 3 months. That sort of whiplash is usually not seen outside of the crypto space and for it to happen to such an established name as Oracle alludes to just how fragile their place in the AI sector can be. This turmoil also rippled far beyond Oracle, exposing emerging systemic risks in the AI sector where financial interconnections and investor sentiment create a domino effect. On the day of the plunge, the Nasdaq Composite fell sharply, dragged down by AI-linked stocks: GE Vernova tumbled nearly 8%, Broadcom shed 3%, and Nvidia dipped 2%, with ASML, TSMC, and AMD also declining amid The S&P 500's top 10 stocks, which now comprise 41% of its total market cap, amplified the fallout, Bad news for one player--whether it be Oracle's financing snag or broader questions about AI profitability--quickly infects the ecosystem. Hyperscalers like Google and Meta depend on shared suppliers and infrastructure, while investor psychology regarding "AI" tie all their fates together: a retreat in Oracle's cloud ambitions casts shadows on chipmakers and hyperscalers alike. Compounding these issues is a growing skepticism about the data center model itself, as AI's rapid evolution threatens to render planned massive structural investments obsolete. Billionaire real estate developer Fernando de Leon, who built a $10 billion empire through Leon Capital Group, has emerged as a vocal critic, warning that the sector's hype ignores fundamental flaws. In a recent interview, de Leon pointed to the absence of big exits for multibillion-dollar centers, noting no comparable sales above $4-5 billion He also questioned why trillion-dollar hyperscalers refuse to own these assets, instead choosing to push the risks onto investors: " The AI business is everything for them today, for the large hyperscalers, and so they're saying, 'No, you build it, you finance it.'" De Leon also highlighted tech obsolescence, arguing that AI's self-improving efficiency will devalue the equipment inside, where the true worth lies--not the buildings and campuses. Moreover, he described long-term leases as " Swiss cheese," riddled with escape clauses that endanger the pension fund-backed capital - hungry for high returns above all else - that have been ploughed in to fund these ventures and could end up holding the bag. As AI models grow more efficient--requiring less compute for greater output--these gargantuan facilities could become white elephants, with billions left to rot in stranded assets. Oracle's saga along this rocky road over the next few months will illuminate a lot about the AI boom's long term sustainability, as hyped projections meet economic gravity. If even a veteran like Oracle stumbles under their incurred debt's weight, what does that mean for startups chasing similar dreams? Will an industry that is so tightly interconnected heed the warnings, or chase the mirage further into the desert? This moment could herald a recalibration, where innovation tempers with prudence, or it might presage a broader correction, popping the bubble of unchecked optimism. Whatever the outcome, we have been afforded a taste of the wider market consequences this week. The emerging systemic risk in the AI industry means there will be A LOT of collateral damage if something goes wrong. Oracle is however still a major player, that they will be instrumental in the long running TikTok deal negotiations is at least a bright spot on the horizon. Shares are currently up 8% on the year, but the fact that they were up as high as 95% and then lost it all can't be ignored. Oracle is staying true to its name sake. It is revealing hidden knowledge to us, but it turns out that what we are learning is that true progress demands not just mystical vision, but viable economic foundations. The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left Wing Journalism You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 The Capitalist 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

name: Nellie Pou
subject: Republicans don't have a plan
body:
And it's why we must take back the House

| |

Hi friend, The price of everything is skyrocketing, and now health care premiums are set to explode. And what is the House Republican majority doing? Nothing. No plan. No solutions. No interest in helping the families they represent. Instead, they’re raising prices with tariffs that are leaving grocery shelves empty. They’re ignoring the utility spikes. They’re letting health care premiums soar while blocking every attempt to lower drug costs. And they’re using their power to attack our rights, not cut costs. Working families deserve better. We need an agenda focused on real solutions: lowering health care costs, reducing energy bills, strengthening Medicare and Medicaid, and finally giving families room to breathe. That is not happening under the House Republican leadership. Our fight starts with taking back the House. And that starts right here in New Jersey. This district, NJ-09, is a true swing seat. name: Troy Downing
subject: I put so much hope into my last message - but it didn't work.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
I hoped & prayed for a huge response, but it wasn't enough.

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTUwJmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIyMV9DUC1ORC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3Nzc1MjAtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMjFfQ1AtTkQuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzc3NTIwLTI4OTkifQ/Avny6-WF-a8NBP706YBNdQ)**This is the email I've waited all my life to send.**
I put so much hope into my last message - but it didn't work.
I hoped & prayed for a huge response, but it wasn't enough.
**Peter, I'm Troy Downing.** I bet you've never heard of me, but that's OK. I never thought in a million years I'd be sending you this final message pleading for your help.
I'm a combat veteran. A God-fearing Christian. A husband & a proud dad.

**[I'm also the conservative Republican running for re-election for Congress in Montana in the biggest race on the map.](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTUwJmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIyMV9DUC1ORC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3Nzc1MjAtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMjFfQ1AtTkQuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzc3NTIwLTI4OTkifQ/vgADRe3gX1CgEFaI6qk11g)**

Phony Republicans & the DC swamp have sold you out. That's NOT me. I'm making a simple promise to everyone reading this message:******I'll always tell the truth. ****
****I'll always put America First. ****
****I'll always have a backbone.** This is exactly why the lefties in Washington will smear me in the media, lie about me & end my run. They already have a $40 million in cash on hand to destroy every Republican like me.
I am swallowing my pride & pleading for your help. I'm not used to asking like this, so here it goes: [Do you have the heart to chip in just a few bucks to help me?](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTUwJmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIyMV9DUC1ORC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3Nzc1MjAtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMjFfQ1AtTkQuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzc3NTIwLTI4OTkifQ/p95OSC3gCg3iZsi8zceU6g)****[ENLIST TODAY
](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTUwJmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIyMV9DUC1ORC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3Nzc1MjAtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMjFfQ1AtTkQuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzc3NTIwLTI4OTkifQ/e5mSC0v-BaMZG8v_Paf9RA) I don't have the flashy campaign slogans or the high-tech stuff - I'm taking a leap of faith & praying that a few people read this message & are willing to stand with me.

Most people will count me out, but if we WIN this race it will bring the Radical Left to its knees.

I'm praying for a comeback, Peter - [will you help a combat vet like me?](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTUwJmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIyMV9DUC1ORC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3Nzc1MjAtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMjFfQ1AtTkQuMTEwMTE3X3QxNzc3NTIwLTI4OTkifQ/tbxePwcct-xzj4ZIVs9vQg)****[ENLIST TODAY

This email is unlike anything I've ever sent you before.

No, this isn't about a campaign or an election.

I'm sending you an urgent message about an organization I'm personally supporting, The Atlas Society.

Their CEO, Jennifer Grossman, has been my friend for thirty years -- we got our start in cable news together in the mid-1990s, and she later hired my firm to do polling when she worked at Dole Food Company. Through ups and downs, we've remained friends.

[](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=c7fdb010c61e479cb6a6407b69e7cb25&_e=BtbyPBlte1lPPYhH1TYvdGS_gdZq-qbUZKWmMv1hOiwM7e5r98mQXqWhf1vwvv3VrjTVJbyxdfnAb0KOO8ijwkDkpHGbhO5qMaJEzU-DQqbfxiBf-9YNT3c-zHBIfFLeDv5ex1BVY2ilhOS5Xj6rvaZcPf8pEqPOaG1rEnqujg2yQorO6uAlOuG7LlWuQmRrBL-geTLPjEa607Txh4wX2wTgzSOFvVGUqlTgyKTHfDNNaHDp8u7PUuk7ygZMnmLiBl14g2XjeKbq_tuTged0X4hb-hLsr-oe9MqyP_wGE3ABB4O7uy0yAody1wELl3BniQRUGBfSWdI-B1fafHeho9dNAEXR4Q5WgG1V9pD-wazzdQCwFjCP_66DDPYWWZ2uJpTgV-Ea9TVK_4N_kegwXY1tlG_Pus0KtyK0oZK3jFHakjSebF0WircA1e8d-EIS5kg17-JatlV85PmMpCnOQ7-f0yw3RkRjQgbrLsEZjQSrKP3ztvGZdnH4DyD6pZaguQ5WZ57Eff8NvQF0kdEC5MUhLuTp90t_k3IFc5QCi0FDt0Scs_7qRhjogbqeN1JTIHtWTMsBzqT9lck1wUmquQPO0JSA4JBIEotCLXexEcFcDQFqSx6q5DA5-FztARSRULqG4IwJx38xhWwqvbgAnQYAsZAPboI5cr807Ul2jG5qMMZa0vdJwjNUDqSdn8G3dUUUBcF8d_F15I8CDn61WYNSip5ew5zHFuKkl3UBAUuNg4JaGCY6OHHLHP0kc1AgONwHatjCl8RoIqM9p45b98DShIHaiZsHvexS2Fk7U50%3D)

Today, she's the CEO of The Atlas Society, a non-profit organization whose mission is to spread Ayn Rand's message of capitalism, personal responsibility, reason, and entrepreneurship to young people. **[I promised Jennifer I would help her raise $25,000 to support the mission. Please click here to donate $10, $50, $100, $500, $1,000, or ANY amount >>>](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=c7fdb010c61e479cb6a6407b69e7cb25&_e=BtbyPBlte1lPPYhH1TYvdGS_gdZq-qbUZKWmMv1hOiwM7e5r98mQXqWhf1vwvv3VrjTVJbyxdfnAb0KOO8ijwkDkpHGbhO5qMaJEzU-DQqbfxiBf-9YNT3c-zHBIfFLeDv5ex1BVY2ilhOS5Xj6rvaZcPf8pEqPOaG1rEnqujg1vpKJCcwThFWdLj6faX6yjg-xqtGMz-nJRG1fIPlDuIqmka2Brx59KDOnX3hVzB80MV3052O-UTXYdh6y4B9qpdGPIlyqWJKk0M3X2DIYFdariaqaeRsrQz3K_ReyUe3XsagAS9KpKqFQJ4bzfTy1HWtalW7FNlUabau64USBWB6dzEqEzLEonwwo-DbgMWdYDXiYKutAlxHgLKp5yTggdn7TQaZkGny7-COqoutP1ou6RPDDtlXmlxPuPmSAUT6SpL6YrephqzUvHcnpIzxvqEcWUztD31svEUpUHMUGNIr_L7WbAKYQ1uHkaXAR60KGsKbhp8dXuuTfYWcfs_Ljyjo21wW7NCfcnHF8V81scJISHof1O93e1qohHNV2zJD5GkeImwSQTTBrCqVktq_AXnvyAlJPcw98R64YtfEWAYn9FAWN81C3Nyh8oEZtBakg2mmAX0NZwUgr5A8ncKtj_sqQxKVzHxG--Sk5cm7PpUcHPajjrY8vFcpvxc9pPqsgDQK9VNdt3yYTBmmhbbtTIcgcZvmu2UonnTU47dcsYeG5bL_OmBtKxDqsrfBTCK0zcaF_9P_cakFWEIPvtUtiCxjB9gkXHoCELlegUCCxG9a2OiqRHBE-5CL3znpqq9sPNS3DwR9O221WqNOKw1pL6)**

Why is The Atlas Society's mission so important?

If my career in polling and serving as President Trump's 2016 Campaign Manager taught me anything, it's this: Americans are _**desperate**_ to hear the unfiltered truth.

**This is why we need more young people to hear The Atlas Society 's message. Here is what Jennifer and her organization are fighting for:**

* Free market capitalism
* Respect for achievement and success
* Free speech
* The future of young people and our great country

Our culture is pushing a twisted message of victimization, envy, woke nonsense, socialism, class warfare, and hatred for our country and its founding principles.

Young people need an alternative.

The Atlas Society spreads Ayn Rand's message to millions of young people via social media, video content, graphic novels, conferences, and so much more.

I promised Jennifer I would help her raise $25,000 for The Atlas Society, which is why I am reaching out to you. Please consider making a donation by using one of the links I've copied below:

clean_body: This email is unlike anything I've ever sent you before. No, this isn't about a campaign or an election. I'm sending you an urgent message about an organization I'm personally supporting, The Atlas Society. Their CEO, Jennifer Grossman, has been my friend for thirty years -- we got our start in cable news together in the mid-1990s, and she later hired my firm to do polling when she worked at Dole Food Company. Through ups and downs, we've remained friends. Today, she's the CEO of The Atlas Society, a non-profit organization whose mission is to spread Ayn Rand's message of capitalism, personal responsibility, reason, and entrepreneurship to young people. Why is The Atlas Society's mission so important? If my career in polling and serving as President Trump's 2016 Campaign Manager taught me anything, it's this: Americans are desperate to hear the unfiltered truth. This is why we need more young people to hear The Atlas Society 's message. Here is what Jennifer and her organization are fighting for: Free market capitalism Respect for achievement and success Free speech The future of young people and our great country Our culture is pushing a twisted message of victimization, envy, woke nonsense, socialism, class warfare, and hatred for our country and its founding principles. Young people need an alternative. The Atlas Society spreads Ayn Rand's message to millions of young people via social media, video content, graphic novels, conferences, and so much more. I promised Jennifer I would help her raise $25,000 for The Atlas Society, which is why I am reaching out to you. Please consider making a donation by using one of the links I've copied below: You may be surprised, but when young people hear Ayn Rand's message of entrepreneurship, refusing to be a victim, and personal achievement, they are inspired and want to learn more. Yes, politics are important. But politics are downstream from culture. And culture is downstream from philosophy. The Atlas Society is on the frontlines of fighting in the philosophical culture war, which may determine the fate of the country's future. We CANNOT give up the culture or give up young people. This is more important than a campaign or a single election. You and I must invest in reaching young people with this message. Please don't wait to take action. My goal is to raise this $25,000 ASAP within a 48-hour period. Can I count on you to make a donation? Kellyanne Conway, Former Senior Counselor to President Trump The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible. 1038 Miwok Dr Lodi, CA 95240

clean_body: | | | Peter, If you just opened this email, I hope you read this message very carefully. This is Representative Bill Huizenga. The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC) is launching an all-out FUNDRAISING BLITZ against my campaign, flooding my district with MILLIONS of dollars in a desperate attempt to buy this seat and silence YOUR voice. Why would they target me in Michigan? Because they know that if Republicans like you and me LOSE this battleground seat in battleground Michigan ... Our Republican majority in the House will CRUMBLE – and with it CRASHES DOWN President Trump’s entire America First agenda. Peter , we already know the Democrats will do anything to SEIZE ABSOLUTE POWER and OBLITERATE your voice in Congress... The Democrat lobbyists would love nothing more than to INSTALL another puppet politician. The Far-Left Extremists would love to take us back to the Pelosi-Biden years of OPEN BORDERS , DEVASTATING CRIME , and SKYROCKETING PRICES. That’s why the DCCC is spending MILLIONS to attack my character, my voting record, and the conservative values you and I stand for. Yes, they’ve got the entire D.C. fundraising machine behind them. Yes, they have an entire army of lawyers and campaign consultants on speed dial... But what they do NOT have is the support of real American voters. And that’s what matters most. I’m counting on YOU to help defend my critical battleground seat by rushing over $5. Seriously, just $5. Your support doesn’t go unnoticed. Thank you, Rep. Bill Huizenga | | PAID FOR BY HUIZENGA FOR CONGRESS PO BOX 245 Zeeland, MI, 49464

name: Team In This Together PAC
subject: Big news: Andy is now leading the DGA
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
This is no small task.

Peter,

It's official — Gov. Andy Beshear is now the Chair of the Democratic Governors Association!

That means he's stepping into one of the most important roles in Democratic politics: **Making sure more Democratic Governors are elected to lead states across the country with common sense.**

_That's no small task._

As Chair, Andy will be helping to reelect Democratic Governors who are already serving their states, electing new Democratic Governors in states with retiring incumbents, and working to flip governor’s seats in states currently led by Republicans.

That means supporting strong, principled candidates who will lead with dignity and respect for their people and fighting to protect Democratic incumbents _that the GOP will be doing everything in its power to kick out of office._

He’ll be making sure Democrats stay focused on what matters most: Listening to voters, solving real problems, and delivering ideas and solutions that actually improve people's lives.

Andy is excited to take on this new responsibility, but his work with In This Together PAC isn't slowing down, not for a minute. Because while he's helping Democrats win in gubernatorial races nationwide, we're still laser-focused on supporting candidates in tough districts up and down the ballot who are committed to making life better for the people they represent.

We need you with us. Every single race, every single candidate, every single voter we reach depends on folks stepping up when it counts.

clean_body: This is no small task. Peter, It's official — Gov. Andy Beshear is now the Chair of the Democratic Governors Association! That means he's stepping into one of the most important roles in Democratic politics: Making sure more Democratic Governors are elected to lead states across the country with common sense. That's no small task. As Chair, Andy will be helping to reelect Democratic Governors who are already serving their states, electing new Democratic Governors in states with retiring incumbents, and working to flip governor’s seats in states currently led by Republicans. That means supporting strong, principled candidates who will lead with dignity and respect for their people and fighting to protect Democratic incumbents that the GOP will be doing everything in its power to kick out of office. He’ll be making sure Democrats stay focused on what matters most: Listening to voters, solving real problems, and delivering ideas and solutions that actually improve people's lives. Andy is excited to take on this new responsibility, but his work with In This Together PAC isn't slowing down, not for a minute. Because while he's helping Democrats win in gubernatorial races nationwide, we're still laser-focused on supporting candidates in tough districts up and down the ballot who are committed to making life better for the people they represent. We need you with us. Every single race, every single candidate, every single voter we reach depends on folks stepping up when it counts. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, Team In This Together PAC | Andy and In This Together PAC are working to support leaders who will lead with compassion and decency, reject anger politics, and always do what's right. name: Beth Farnham for Congress
subject: District Round-up 12.21.2025
body:
Dear Neighbor, Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been...
---
| | | | [Beth Farnham for Congress](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com&sr=0&signature=234e71905ce6fc7ea848d3ba9fc507a0&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z) | [Read on blog](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2025%2F12%2F21%2Fdistrict-round-up-12-21-2025%2F&sr=0&signature=6549a8da24eff91be8cae3ee492a97f1&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z) or [Reader](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wordpress.com%2Freader%2Fblogs%2F213069660%2Fposts%2F5991&sr=0&signature=d9d76852b7ea3528f90a8a6914492340&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
---|---|---
# [District Round-up 12.21.2025](https://public-api.wordpress.com/bar/?stat=groovemails-events&bin=wpcom_email_click&redirect_to=http%3A%2F%2Fbethfarnhamforcongress.com%2F2025%2F12%2F21%2Fdistrict-round-up-12-21-2025%2F&sr=0&signature=6549a8da24eff91be8cae3ee492a97f1&blog_id=213069660&user=248418217&_&_z=z)
---
By **Elizabeth Farnham** on December 21, 2025
---
| Dear Neighbor,
---
Thank you for reading my District Round-up. As your candidate for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District, it is my honor and pleasure to deliver to you where I’ve been and what issues I’ve tackled in the past week. Criss-crossing the counties of Adams, Franklin, Fulton, Bedford, Cambria, Blair, Huntingdon, Mifflin, Juniata, Perry, Cumberland, and Somerset gives me great opportunities to meet with voters, hear what is on your hearts and minds, and the chance to voice my fight for Everyday Americans. If you would like to contribute to my campaign, please donate here: <https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beth-farnham-for-congress-1>
---
|
---
During this holiday season, I hope that you and your loved ones enjoy your time together. As I turn more of my attention to a celebration of my faith and family, I am reminded of the tenets many of us hold dear – to feed the hungry, heal the sick, help the poor, and welcome the stranger. It is in this spirit that Team Beth Farnham for Congress will be helping out at the Gettysburg Holiday Dinner on Tuesday. May we all feel the warmth of the holiday and have hope renewed for a better year.
---
I am grateful to the wonderful team I am working with this year and into next. If you haven’t gotten the chance, please check them out at [Meet the Team!](https://bethfarnhamforcongress.com/5868-2/). They’re a great group of people with tremendous drive, experience, and reliability. And if you would like to volunteer for the campaign, I’ll have an even better update next week! Exciting things to come!
---
|
---
Photo credit: Sandy Hartzell
---
Yesterday, I was proud to march with others in support of Mr. Carlos Bonilla, who had been deported in a harsh way. I thank the organizers who put this together.
---
“CHAMBERSBURG, Pa. — Dozens of people marched through Chambersburg on Saturday morning following the deportation of Carlos Bonilla, a longtime community member who was detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement last month and later deported to Mexico.” [March Held in Chambersburg](https://www.fox43.com/article/news/politics/march-held-in-chambersburg-following-deportation-of-longtime-resident/521-c9678e4d-d322-4d0f-8eee-dc9626d5412d)
---
The thing is, while Mr. Carlos did commit the crime of entering the country illegally once upon a time, he was a productive member of society, having worked here for decades, paying taxes, and having built his family. Our tax dollars do not need to be used to demonize, round up, and deport people like him just for reaching for a piece of the American dream. Just fine them, grant them citizenship, and use our tax dollars for something that improves our lives, like Universal Healthcare. In other words, let us actually welcome the stranger.
---
Until that happens, it is important that we keep showing up and speaking out about the egregious overreach of ICE, especially when it comes to established and beloved members of the community.
---
Many thanks to Natalie Koranda for having interviewed me.
---
“Beth Farnham, a candidate for the U.S. House of Representatives for Pennsylvania’s 13th Congressional District attended the march spoke in support of Bonilla. ‘I did not know Mr. Carlos personally. I was touched by his personal story. He’s a beloved member of the community he serves, and I’m so grateful that the rest of the community turned out for him,’ she said.”
---
| [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RU2DdwIC1Pw)
---
Unfortunately, our Commander in Chief does not share in this spirit. Not only is his administration kidnapping people without due process in The United States of America, he is fanning the flames of war internationally.
---
And so I raise the question – what happens when a rogue President of the United States unilaterally directs a naval blockade of another country which is unquestionably an act of war?
---
Well, if you’re our US Representative, multimillionaire John Joyce, then the answer is...absolutely nothing.
---
In John Joyce’s world, the President IS the Congress who has the Constitutional power to declare war.
---
But for those of us in reality, only the US Congress, actually representative of Everyday Americans, i.e. The People, can authorize hostilities within or against Venezuela.
---
Mercifully, those members of Congress who understand their jobs are fast-tracking to the floor a recorded vote on whether or not Trump must end those hostilities. And considering that up to this point Venezuela has not been attacking the United States militarily, but the US has been “double tapping” supposed drug dealers with the full force of US military might at Trump’s direction despite no evidence of drug dealing, it is imperative that members of Congress vote to stop these international war crimes.
---
Of course, we can’t trust Joyce to vote the right way, the way of the Constitution, because he is such a bootlicker for Trump. But we can vote him out of office on November 3rd, 2026.
---
Please vote for me, Beth Farnham, instead, because I understand and would uphold my oath regarding which branch of government holds which powers.
---
Together, we [#ChooseDemocracy](https://www.facebook.com/hashtag/choosedemocracy?__cft__%5B0%5D=AZYmPYlYHCFVPvO3QF6Y7WV03JudhDDSC2eRAYae-_LQjmJaDYQ6N3JuYqpjOI5KfSl-b0T9UQtyo4My2pgI9FRco5ASmOjXr27VTSqLw7iVbVic41-kcnlyG2iN2dD9mYB4ig2sP4MpH7LthIEXkbMPqaAYmoekb8RMzBjZzACkklxOf-a5xGVB8Zf0gdJmoJeXShkOCj-0iHxT8RHiVHKj&__tn__=*NK-R) and the US Constitution!
---
|
---
Photo credit: Jack Stansbury
---
Many thanks to the Perry County Democrats for hosting their holiday potluck. I enjoyed speaking with them about the issues of our district and our plans for next year.
---
If you’re like me, you love this country as much as I do and work to make a positive difference. In the midst of this Constitutional Crisis, please consider reaching out to your local Democratic committees or Democratic candidates so that we can strengthen our Democracy instead of letting it slide into fascism.
---
We don’t just #ChooseDemocracy, we choose Pennsylvania, we choose The United States of America, we choose Humanity and I am deeply glad to work along side you.
---
Sincerely,
---
Beth
---
| [Like](http://bethfarnhamforcongress.com/2025/12/21/district-round-up-12-21-2025/?like_actor=248418217&like_hmac=229e3c9741e259d6c541c677f6cdbef4&postid=5991&like=1&source=sub_email)
---

name: Team Trump
subject: What’s your current level of satisfaction with President Donald Trump?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump National Committee JFC, Inc
body:
We’d like to submit your response to Trump before midnight!

subject: The book Charlie left behind
body:
Charlie’s final message—support Turning Point USA & receive his last book.
| | [ ](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=cde3487ad8334eee9f8ad37ec6521598&_e=KB6KR-dRmm-e-ToOjRkOsjgTyF_gS49GERuCMs9pDvEfyKWJdClRwAMYWGL19EsIi2KJDya2U0FKfFy6mIi7AZSbg5ERrYS0nTr6lBDL5gLuPxCUX_d1S6L6_29dAD23pl_LwDjeccDjSVeMy4heN272RK3zqatV5n9cJ0QDnDkmC42u3odSrh_aTisFfhGIcXWb8uIFi107dW4-VdOmUqQP9ieeq0QEU9bxF1AyjYxySQ2Y-Xg5JE9MM49TGAccYmEgh3IZUUt4_ylmr41LlWJABaVb3MyEnwCx29K01dLXfJhUeWNPOrv3H2LfDN7CYF9HgyQoHIXIw5EKFN8xTSDtWs5wDk12xLOXv1V4BC83JMK24RBBe4DfhLalJG4Nhy7DlBpo7iCA0bWwRUU7zEjty-wnBOfuGE-Gwy33CxRNpmyF8Ca8QZSaWP_hKV3Ec-bQAyRxdZu5uUa-WzFNCkfbIzkDGcOqV-IT4KZzyjCHRZW7pCYe61tqcedh1aWYp-Uo1t5qRw7m2NaDM8YF--cdnK1uFXNZGSFKvtd0F7lw6bOIIee7Zq1n9S-Sn3gL1nkCq6H2owYjhTYagWnyL33knoQRzVHMamM3TXqPuyLBoCLOEGXcv3WGDVyxKNNZfVqfTVhbLohphF5wr5-BexZDvliJ8SvIwZHumb4lRLF3olv3jI2wmm5belh9qI_v)
---
| Fellow Patriot,
---
| Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud.

He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus.

He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness.

And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible.

I saw it up close -- _Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. _

But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything.

_**He started observing the Sabbath. **_

Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance.

_But as an act of obedience. _

As a way of saying, **_" God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." _**

Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped.

He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield.

No email. No media. No work.

He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children.

It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much.

But over time, it changed him.

And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned...

name: Kris Mayes
subject: Trump is abandoning Arizona
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY
body:
Last week, I talked with MS NOW about how the Trump administration is abandoning Arizona's fight against drug cartels. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121734597/590405952/-1397648958?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_TAN_VIP_AC_20251221_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTJmMjJjOWQtOWRkZS1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=iUrMjBcdh-rD_jtzjKGW9nWyFZ6osNJ1GHRM5eIIHBc=)
---
Hi there, It's Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes. As you may have heard me say before, Arizona is the fentanyl funnel for America. _More than half of all fentanyl seized in this country comes through my state._ And we've seized more than 27 million pills since I became Attorney General. **But here’s the thing: Attorney General Pam Bondi and Trump are abandoning Arizona's fight against drug cartels.** After I sent a letter to Bondi requesting an additional 50 DEA agents for Arizona**,** she actually took 60 DEA agents OUT of Arizona. I wrote an op-ed and went on MS NOW to tell this story: [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121734598/590405953/727665197?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_TAN_VIP_AC_20251221_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTJmMjJjOWQtOWRkZS1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=iUrMjBcdh-rD_jtzjKGW9nWyFZ6osNJ1GHRM5eIIHBc=) Bondi moved these agents to places like Washington, D.C., so they could patrol Foggy Bottom, pick up trash, and terrorize immigrants. That is not what we need. We have one out of every 10 prosecutors now doing immigration enforcement when they should be going after drug cartels. Federal drug prosecutions are down 10%. CBP checkpoints along major trafficking routes are going unattended. **And meanwhile, Trump pardoned the former President of Honduras, who bragged about stuffing "drugs up the gringos' noses" after flooding 500 tons of cocaine into the U.S.** This is no laughing matter. More than 40% of Americans know someone who died from a drug overdose. Arizona is doing our part every day to fight the cartels and stop the flow of fentanyl. But we can't do this alone. clean_body: Last week, I talked with MS NOW about how the Trump administration is abandoning Arizona's fight against drug cartels. | = Hi there, It's Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes. As you may have heard me say before, Arizona is the fentanyl funnel for America. More than half of all fentanyl seized in this country comes through my state. And we've seized more than 27 million pills since I became Attorney General. But here’s the thing: Attorney General Pam Bondi and Trump are abandoning Arizona's fight against drug cartels. After I sent a letter to Bondi requesting an additional 50 DEA agents for Arizona, she actually took 60 DEA agents OUT of Arizona. I wrote an op-ed and went on MS NOW to tell this story: = Bondi moved these agents to places like Washington, D.C., so they could patrol Foggy Bottom, pick up trash, and terrorize immigrants. That is not what we need. We have one out of every 10 prosecutors now doing immigration enforcement when they should be going after drug cartels. Federal drug prosecutions are down 10%. CBP checkpoints along major trafficking routes are going unattended. And meanwhile, Trump pardoned the former President of Honduras, who bragged about stuffing "drugs up the gringos' noses" after flooding 500 tons of cocaine into the U.S. This is no laughing matter. More than 40% of Americans know someone who died from a drug overdose. Arizona is doing our part every day to fight the cartels and stop the flow of fentanyl. But we can't do this alone. Now, I'm asking Trump and Bondi to return those agents to Arizona and do their actual jobs. We need federal law enforcement focused on actual criminals — cartel members and drug dealers. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to the Arizona Democratic Party and Kris Mayes will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = I'll keep calling out the Trump administration when they abandon Arizona's fight against deadly drugs. Thanks for having my back, \- Kris Arizona Democratic Party PO Box 36123 Phoenix, AZ 85067-6123 United States mailto:info@azdem.org | PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY. NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. GREG FREEMAN, TREASURER. WWW.AZDEM.ORG

name: Chris Taylor for Justice
subject: BREAKING: Elon Musk is donating to far-right campaigns once again
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Taylor for Justice
body:
This could change the entire dynamic of our race.
# [](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/18?refcode=em20251221-932&t=1&akid=940%2E294397%2EiB9bOY)
Breaking news: **Elon Musk is back.**
[](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251221_1_ak.FAtXqp&amount=10&refcode=em20251221-932&t=3&akid=940%2E294397%2EiB9bOY)
This could change the entire dynamic of our race.
We don’t yet know whether Elon Musk is funneling money into our opponent’s campaign — but in just 10 days, on December 31, we’ll find out.
**That’s because December 31 marks our first public fundraising deadline.** Both campaigns will be required to file reports after that showing exactly how much money we’ve raised, and where it came from.
We’ll be able to see if Elon Musk is meddling in Wisconsin’s Supreme Court election once again, and far-right billionaires like him will be able to review our numbers.
They’ll be looking for _any_ sign of weakness.
That’s why we must show that Judge Chris Taylor has a real grassroots coalition behind her — one strong enough to stand up to far-right billionaire meddling in Wisconsin’s Supreme Court race.
[**Rush a $10 donation now before our December 31 deadline:**](https://act.chrisforjustice.com/go/31?c0=em_ms_jct_20251221_1_ak.FAtXqp&amount=10&refcode=em20251221-932&t=4&akid=940%2E294397%2EiB9bOY)

Team Taylor

name: Seojin Ha, OLCV Youth Intern
subject: OLCV has reshaped my future with your help
body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=FsUlb2APSxKahp1qQPcJV7HkhPPST8mCwYjWeeF7JBFnwDBDGCR/uunEACjWuVdbkOktbbaJObvs8ySyqGRuuBbWhG/SX2mf1CfwPjpyusoIi/MyTRas5uVvumSzKATM [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=FsUlb2APSxKahp1qQPcJV7HkhPPST8mCwYjWeeF7JBFnwDBDGCR/uunEACjWuVdbkOktbbaJObvs8ySyqGRuuBbWhG/SX2mf1CfwPjpyusoIi/MyTRas5uVvumSzKATM]
Hi there,
My name is Seojin Ha. I joined OLCV as an intern this past summer. Before I started, I was an average young person, and as a teenager in 2025, I felt confused. Much of the world’s problems seemed to be an “adult” topic, not something us teenagers needed to know. It often felt like young people did not have a strong voice in the issues we care about, especially the environment: I mean look at the U.S. Congress. How many of our legislators truly represent the younger generation? I wanted to change that for myself; I wanted to grow my confidence in fighting for what I believe in: fighting for our earth. So, the OLCV internship seemed like an obvious next step.
Through this internship, I have seen how powerful our voices can be. By advocating for the recent transportation package, I saw firsthand that our advocacy is not symbolic. The idea that volunteers, activists, and communities can actually affect how our world functions blew me away.
Without your support, my internship with OLCV would not have been possible. I’m humbly asking you to make an end-of-year gift to OLCV so that other young people like me can have this formative opportunity that teaches you how to be a climate activist. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2]
Make a gift [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2?am=50&contactdata=FsUlb2APSxKahp1qQPcJV7HkhPPST8mCwYjWeeF7JBFnwDBDGCR/uunEACjWuVdbkOktbbaJObvs8ySyqGRuuBbWhG/SX2mf1CfwPjpyusoIi/MyTRas5uVvumSzKATM]
This internship has reshaped how I see my role in Oregon’s future. As a member of the younger generation who will soon be responsible for solving many of these challenges, I now understand that environmental politics is not something that happens in a room in D.C., it happens on the streets. OLCV has shown me that when we are engaged and informed about climate policy, we become part of the reason it changes for the better.
Will you make a generous gift now so that OLCV’s youth internship program can continue? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/t28-fgcXRUmjEwNoP96SgQ2]
Thank you,
Seojin Ha (he/him)
OLCV Youth Intern
P.S.: We are in the big push for end of year fundraising. If you would no longer like to receive emails related to end of year fundraising, please fill out this form here to opt-out [https://secure.everyaction.com/c2_U_ki-NEyU8Gdkttquug2] but still remain on our general email list!

1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214
United States

name: Voter Action Project
subject: Send a thank you card to our postal workers this holiday season
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Folks,
Postal workers have continued to deliver for us through a particularly brutal year.
Their official motto is “neither snow nor rain nor heat nor gloom of night” will stop the Postal Service, but they also have delivered through aggressive political attacks, job cuts, service slowdowns, and nonstop pressure from Donald Trump, who calls the USPS a “tremendous loser” and tried to shove it under the Commerce Department and fire the board to pave the way for privatization.
Yet they deliver for all of us. Let’s deliver messages of gratitude directly to postal workers across the country, on their routes, in sorting centers, at distribution hubs, and in local post offices.
Sign the card and add your words of support to thank our postal workers across the country today >> [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
During the holiday season, their busiest time of the year, USPS workers move over 15 billion pieces of mail and packages, including more than 400 million packages in a single week, to every single ZIP code in the country.
Today, we can boost their spirits, and show them that the country values their service. When you sign, you can also write a personal note to share with workers. Our coalition partners at the American Postal Workers Union will deliver our messages of thanks directly to postal workers across the country.
Add your name and a personal message of thanks by clicking here >> [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=122125_1&amount=20]

clean_body: Voter Action Project [] Folks, Postal workers have continued to deliver for us through a particularly brutal year. Their official motto is “neither snow nor rain nor heat nor gloom of night” will stop the Postal Service, but they also have delivered through aggressive political attacks, job cuts, service slowdowns, and nonstop pressure from Donald Trump, who calls the USPS a “tremendous loser” and tried to shove it under the Commerce Department and fire the board to pave the way for privatization. Yet they deliver for all of us. Let’s deliver messages of gratitude directly to postal workers across the country, on their routes, in sorting centers, at distribution hubs, and in local post offices. Sign the card and add your words of support to thank our postal workers across the country today >> [] SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [] During the holiday season, their busiest time of the year, USPS workers move over 15 billion pieces of mail and packages, including more than 400 million packages in a single week, to every single ZIP code in the country. Today, we can boost their spirits, and show them that the country values their service. When you sign, you can also write a personal note to share with workers. Our coalition partners at the American Postal Workers Union will deliver our messages of thanks directly to postal workers across the country. Add your name and a personal message of thanks by clicking here >> [] SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [] Voter Action Project [] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. name: Haley Stevens
subject: How are you? (I genuinely want to know)

body:
this will only take a moment, but it's important

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p)
Hi, null, it's Haley.
---
I want to know: _How was your year? Like, really?
_
We launched a new survey to hear about your 2025, and I truly want to know about your experience.
It will only take one minute, but it couldn't be more important. Your input is critical as I plan what issues to focus on on the campaign trail in 2026. **[Will you be one of the 37 responses we still need?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10734?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=2&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p)**
| | [Take the Survey](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10744?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&%3Bzip=18326&t=3&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p)
---
Thank you!

Haley
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: **Team Haley
** Subject: How was your 2025? We genuinely want to know
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10741?t=4&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p)
---
We want to hear from you, null!
Haley has talked to countless Michiganders on the campaign trail this year, but **we still haven't heard from one very important person: YOU!**
That's why we launched a new short survey to hear about your 2025. Your priorities and feedback are critical to the success of this grassroots campaign – **[will you take a moment and answer a few quick questions about your year?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10734?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p)**
[**We need 75 responses from Cresco before midnight tonight to reach statistical significance. Looking forward to hearing from you, null:**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10734?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p)
| | [Take the Survey](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10734?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=7&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p)
---
Thanks for your help,

Team Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10742?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=8&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10741?t=9&akid=1710%2E49239%2Eq3Ry-p)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

Peter,
Shhh! Don't tell Bushra, but the team is plotting a little holiday magic behind her back. 🤐🎁 I'm Hope, Bushra's Campaign Manager, and I'm coming to you with a top-secret mission.
Right now, Bushra is looking at the numbers, and to be honest, she's worried. Between the holiday shopping rush and end-of-year travel, fundraising has slowed to a crawl. If we don't pick up the pace, this has the potential to be the absolute worst holiday ever for her.
Why? Because if we fall behind on our goals, Bushra won't be spending the holidays relaxing with her family. She'll be glued to her desk at campaign HQ, making grueling "call time" fundraising asks until her voice gives out just to keep us on track.
But the team and I have a better idea: Operation Silent Night ✨
We want to surprise Bushra by hitting our $5,000 midnight goal before she even wakes up tomorrow. Imagine the look on her face when she checks the dashboard and realizes the community has already stepped up!

subject: To the State Supreme Court…

body:
. . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .

---
Friend,
---
On 11/21, the Democrat Attorney General in Arizona had the chance to do the right thing and DROP her wrongful prosecution against me – an _innocent_ 80-year-old man facing 20 years in prison.
---
_But at the _final_ _hour__ , she chose bloodthirsty **VENGEANCE** over **JUSTICE** , and decided to continue her prosecution against me and put me on _TRIAL_ right after the holidays are over.
---
**She is now rushing to the __State__ Supreme Court for emergency assistance.**
---
It’s all viciously calculated. She knows the President has no pardon authority for __state__ crimes.
---
But let me be clear: **I will never give up the fight for my freedom**.
---
When President Trump and I were arrested in the most violent jail in America, we proclaimed, “ _NEVER_ SURRENDER!”
---
Two years later, President Trump is back in the White House.
---
_**But I’m still fighting for my freedom!** And Democrats have RIGGED the case so that a federal pardon from President Trump is **_POWERLESS_** in _state_ court._
---
[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=bfced1d235344d27901063d306e575f6&_e=-D57R5TOGZOyQZ56I1vYmi4367xM-WfSDACr8bStnWlW8Q_j5dxCEytAEwm_dCP2dj9usMh4DX4NLuiDbOw3LYuyqZhUj3eegjXcrbRJbPwHwnq5dhL4g8vgdD6Wbt44F-alSehpL-BE9XjusjnZ0zdeYTczJgYXDlBjy8Rz2fBj6RqsNlvq4pBcuydi4ijx_k_gJdUPNeefYyOBJNPTYMd9CKsW_S3n65NOMxFqfv5QTZF2Hp__9EAdJ9tKCqTc2XuKFgLp37v71RBab3CMtEupsueuezu2OZRInQIgqdaq-sEU9Y9yNInI7FO0Ve9rAvdHqoIPn4Ym-M4nxPmMRS0HWGJAijd83XpuLFV842ZdoGr5aNtemLD59KDM58sDXnC9b3iQlKAs52ty_eCSUljO7I_AIHjxpeoRIUMEjeFvo0wvxE-y9y_WTcigznWqi-lSXVYJb3W69wbEUIrT10FoJLr1cnQ3R5uj0irYCPKkA9rxuWvvBo1Vgi81JBIkVEfJ3WyiyFES3SFOchGZhew2uiRq7u1nlUKnBBziE7DBeGVAEubMOwD7y0S74FMIAlZ6YIYvcyUulsgRDjNNxLaGoXSY7fzjVLmA8Btze1i4i7RjZUlcEmu7Xt4WzFul1tt-kfzMOzlTGDIdw300gg%3D%3D)
__
---
The night I was arrested in the most violent jail in America, the Left filed my mugshot under **booking # 2313757.**
---
_Now, to show these left-wing crooks that good and honest Americans like _YOU_ will not stand by as they destroy the life of an INNOCENT man all for _successfully_ defending President Trump as his lawyer, I’m humbly asking for your support to raise **$231,375.70** for my defense trust to retain a top-notch defense team to fight for my _FREEDOM_._
---
Let me put it bluntly: _you_ truly are my final hope.
---
[**Please make a contribution to the Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund to ensure I have the resources needed to retain a top-notch defense team to WIN what is now the FINAL BATTLE for my freedom and justice.**](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=bfced1d235344d27901063d306e575f6&_e=-D57R5TOGZOyQZ56I1vYmi4367xM-WfSDACr8bStnWlW8Q_j5dxCEytAEwm_dCP2dj9usMh4DX4NLuiDbOw3LYuyqZhUj3eegjXcrbRJbPwHwnq5dhL4g8vgdD6Wbt44F-alSehpL-BE9XjusjnZ0zdeYTczJgYXDlBjy8Rz2fBj6RqsNlvq4pBcuydi4ijx_k_gJdUPNeefYyOBJNPTYG0ELUGB554KXHwRbPjBI4FRMd7OtrTc4Wdx-XFe8Qdmj2hQPN8nsQxQsGAfP7PVOmJgych-OB-6sFpGpFgMbHrfxPFADvytO1cLYuUFsJ7lF5sTvi2KQuV9bsvyqiAO_pJRmq9_0ze6leziumwbEFRPJ0knEfcos0yZyNySjPsR3BapJCuEAElWHvsh99ljPOe9WzJ_ovup-28olyIIdquMCDNL7Rsr5v2joa2DyYIcCqzVYVqNIOdpD-mmGIjlVC6bcqJR88rOd3zimAEiLjcuG6YWD60EHvwlkPz6uZZfGUB8owsfBqIya2G6GY9ZD28APKsi6ZfxWVVNSvSI6k5g96wrMyIDY_a5nTtRr8OW8mNv1T2BlpCL83yPO3p6NE-7f_YSkgMtQ0SSOP6Nze7pjs53RV7KZwlMB11VcQoWrZleFiDMwp1zjgBngxjTug%3D%3D)

We wanted to share an important memo on where things stand in one of the most competitive races in the country and why your continued support is critical as we head into the final stretch of the year.

| [_**We wanted to share an important memo on where things stand in one of the most competitive races in the country and why your continued support is critical in these final 10 days of the year. Please give what you can to Laura’s campaign today. **_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121736745/590412279/800699251?refcode=BLU_EM_LGC_2025-12-21_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NzIwMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzMTk3ZTZmZS1hNWRlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VzCfwlhDofNeMZLXSTCr6PVD7NH_qJDcBLRl215dT7Y=)
---
* * *
**The Big Picture**

Across the country, Democrats are gaining momentum. Recent elections in New Jersey and Virginia showed that voters are rejecting extremism and chaos.

But here on Long Island, the trend is moving in the opposite direction. Republicans are gaining ground locally, and national GOP operatives see flipping this district as one of their best opportunities to hold the House.

**Where Things Stand**
* **The Only Toss-Up in New York:** Nonpartisan analysts at The Cook Political Report continue to rate this district as the state’s only true toss-up.

* **Republican Fundraising Advantage:** Trump and national Republicans have built a record-shattering war chest. For the first time in years, Democrats are being outraised.

* **Relentless GOP Attack Ads:** Republican super PACs are already running negative ads in our district, trying to distort Laura’s record and weaken her standing early.

* **Rising GOP Strength on Long Island:** While Democrats have seen strong results nationally, local Republican gains show this race will be harder than ever to hold.

**What's At Stake**

If Republicans flip this seat, they’ll have an even stronger grip on the House and a clearer path to push their extreme agenda. But if we hold it, Democrats are one step closer to taking back the House — and exposing the corruption, chaos, and abuses of power that have defined this Republican Congress.

**What Laura Needs**

We’re building the infrastructure now to win in 2026, but it’s going to take consistent support to stay competitive. The next few months are critical to:
* Fund our field and voter contact programs

* Counter GOP attack ads already running in the district

* Build the early resources needed to compete against national Republican spending

* * *
We can’t afford to miss a single fundraising goal when so much is at stake.

clean_body: We wanted to share an important memo on where things stand in one of the most competitive races in the country and why your continued support is critical as we head into the final stretch of the year. | = | = The Big Picture Across the country, Democrats are gaining momentum. Recent elections in New Jersey and Virginia showed that voters are rejecting extremism and chaos. But here on Long Island, the trend is moving in the opposite direction. Republicans are gaining ground locally, and national GOP operatives see flipping this district as one of their best opportunities to hold the House. Where Things Stand The Only Toss-Up in New York: Nonpartisan analysts at The Cook Political Report continue to rate this district as the state’s only true toss-up. Republican Fundraising Advantage: Trump and national Republicans have built a record-shattering war chest. For the first time in years, Democrats are being outraised. Relentless GOP Attack Ads: Republican super PACs are already running negative ads in our district, trying to distort Laura’s record and weaken her standing early. Rising GOP Strength on Long Island: While Democrats have seen strong results nationally, local Republican gains show this race will be harder than ever to hold. What's At Stake If Republicans flip this seat, they’ll have an even stronger grip on the House and a clearer path to push their extreme agenda. But if we hold it, Democrats are one step closer to taking back the House — and exposing the corruption, chaos, and abuses of power that have defined this Republican Congress. What Laura Needs We’re building the infrastructure now to win in 2026, but it’s going to take consistent support to stay competitive. The next few months are critical to: Fund our field and voter contact programs Counter GOP attack ads already running in the district Build the early resources needed to compete against national Republican spending We can’t afford to miss a single fundraising goal when so much is at stake. = | | | = | = | = | = | = | | = Paid for and authorized by Gillen for NY. Gillen for NY PO Box 774 Rockville Centre, NY 11571 United States | = If you wish to contribute by check, you can do so at the address above.

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Radical Left Lunatic
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
| | |
---
Derek, at this point, there’s nothing President Trump could say that would shock me. His tirades and rants make the news every day.
_And two weeks ago, he directed one at me._
Trump attacked me personally because he doesn’t like that I stand up to the far-right’s chaos and corruption. Even more than that, Trump is terrified that this campaign can defeat his handpicked candidate here in NC and take back power for the American people.
Derek, I’m not afraid of the president calling me names or riling up his MAGA base against me. But I know that this is just the start of the GOP directing all their attention at us. We’ve seen it time and time again: When Donald Trump attacks a candidate, the GOP money-machine revs up against them, too.
**_[I need your help to face what’s ahead. I’m asking you to split a donation between our campaign and John Fetterman's of whatever you can afford so we can have the resources to fight back](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52438?t=1&akid=10492%2E434096%2EggI_0j)._**
So, you may be wondering what exactly Trump said about me.
He called me a _“radical left lunatic.”_
**If fighting for lower prices, affordable health care, abortion access, voting rights, good schools, and a livable planet makes me a “radical left lunatic,” then so be it.**
You know where I stand and what I’m fighting for, Derek. I need to know if you’re with me.
**_[I’m not asking because I want to, but because I have to. This is the most consequential Senate race on the map. Split a donation between our campaign and John Fetterman's if you can, and help me take on Trump and the national GOP. ](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52438?t=2&akid=10492%2E434096%2EggI_0j)_**
__If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Roy Cooper and John Fetterman will go through immediately:__ | | | [DONATE $5 NOW](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/52435?t=3&akid=10492%2E434096%2EggI_0j)

Roy Cooper

Colorado has become a testing ground for policies that spread nationwide -- soft-on-crime agendas, government overreach, attacks on faith, restrictions on constitutional freedoms. If we stop that tide here, we stop it from sweeping across America. That's why this race is so important. If Colorado flips back, the entire country will feel it. body:
A lifelong Philadelphian, public servant, and fighter for justice. Join Sharif as he brings bold, thoughtful leadership to Washington. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/RNlM66BHF0WVUQpwM4Eu2g2?unsubscribedata=lZHyDOgU2tKu0Z4giEqShtazNmcI5jvCgAD%2BTgSjGTp0vdLTmsNvXbVrK8LhOvmuCEqkus9ILdb1Xhytz%2B%2FpN%2FeH8yc%2Fhqkp7hSRQ6znOs%2BdwvaPFVVHMJNrvKb%2Bl%2Beye7dB7Xoc9SAkI54ndPax1sjxkazE2gluwwtZiV%2Fr8CrouZYIbwTysiDJ%2BS4n3JHsNNzYLNXv7rFnrLBKPeMN50ojkE4N1lo7TeFTfBHiJ132GrTy8ccc7rgHim1fAOQglCHWVxGyuA9csLjs9ZK053zJPawv5NMd39amtXpyYv%2BXJcs7m87vXxK6tD2Lz8KX2sw3Znj8OWqTgRPCsiNcquhvA6j2jVqBamSNHfJpzImhx%2BV5NIrSrjbt4tF8DdlKJe6n%2FduStzgJjOzDLCRCGXLh6CO534YwP7VAJfNMTzZciJ96V5sDjE58UaqLa8%2BO)

Peter,

Philly raised me. Every step of my public service has been about giving back to the community that shaped who I am.

I’ve fought to bring real resources home — funding for violence prevention, healthcare access for working families, and protections for our democracy when it was under attack.

I know what it means to show up when it matters, because I’ve done it time and time again for this city.

Now, as we approach an important end-of-quarter deadline, I’m asking for your help to finish strong. Ending the month on a high note will allow us to keep building, keep organizing, and keep fighting for PA-03.

I’ve fought to bring real resources home — funding for violence prevention, healthcare access for working families, and protections for our democracy when it was under attack. I know what it means to show up when it matters, because I’ve done it time and time again for this city. Now, as we approach an important end-of-quarter deadline, I’m asking for your help to finish strong. Ending the month on a high note will allow us to keep building, keep organizing, and keep fighting for PA-03. = | = With gratitude, Sharif Street * | | | ---|---|---|--- | A lifelong Philadelphian, public servant, and fighter for justice.
