| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

The Pentagon uses it.

And now, even Amazon wants to integrate it into their AI cloud — [alongside Elon’s favorite AI network.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=43808c83710042869d329d2ba5857ad1&_e=OckDvLa5TJkrfuyapd0hWchth6JYiXO_P9c9LjSYSleSJpnCv_pOhVxL-SU1JJuiqn3vYrd7eS63DPksHBd1oj4Xhlt986QSmKileGeiUnc57ivZBRn9h2CLJ0_mF40M6i0xuQdFvWgl7NKL-amNh2ohF-dUohAyYn0xXew9vI7xDUSLfGYXDJe60qj0hOnCLXapOM8-rqx9dgQsc9z8HAI6477NLHItjdcSGs6DlNPiwel4qZlKTLYdWWBbw3ksUFbcDvvg4ie0-QhUxkrMqntNCShvGDkC40c7eJ-hZvI33VjcSn-sEoOjt4NsHd7H1jneJiKf66IJFYDO5awYUFAUDT82C-5uKGvBEaabQ00le2UWUEvnZzmAp-6lXH6HjygZhgRnREz2ZTkVEdmAZ4BfDxl78Axj_xhBu9lGUGuLO8qRRuA_dtVE0iWqTuZGAy9M5TWhQCDv5HRrE3oft3axqrZI1eYvgxktS2nmF3I6X-jy_PiaH0_87kxrr9WYojAACnZelgsDkqdqUVrlJU_Cf6DEBaqFT3TYHwiUOrYuj0MPXQ7eAGVMRrLaulhlR7eRWb9XJ-WabqW_Wvu8qQ%3D%3D)

This same company is still **15 times smaller than Nvidia.**
Nvidia already soared **7,730%**.
This one could be next.

[See why I call it “Elon’s #1 AI stock” — and why it could be the biggest winner of 2025.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=43808c83710042869d329d2ba5857ad1&_e=OckDvLa5TJkrfuyapd0hWchth6JYiXO_P9c9LjSYSleSJpnCv_pOhVxL-SU1JJuiqn3vYrd7eS63DPksHBd1oj4Xhlt986QSmKileGeiUnc57ivZBRn9h2CLJ0_mF40M6i0xuQdFvWgl7NKL-amNh2ohF-dUohAyYn0xXew9vI7xDUSLfGYXDJe60qj0hOnCo_snuOoqT-lhjccaXcxXYQwpCT1sMRs4gLR2w-dURGMe8U78YoaQT-_ISeTBDyPcwD8VElU66VXEbTDMOYhqgiEI9IoutnCL9fAuELvGwAlZ9P6fZEkVCKC5ubIctwM2RbzK4EygIC5kd3Oh3QcWQfWN8MsgfID3A2o5pSOZeoYJ6_srh1Gdv55gGEt6xpMKBf5b2r2i6oOrj8ODVvsMUknRhdBJd9Cv9js1v_18SiE_dsXxoKQ8w3Z8dqmWlhyaESu2nAq8YSXiMJKxssKG3qCF2tt_8gprbipElnbSFL8ay4XCrnjUS4xETCPGyxWjghsiW9qAtM-wS3DCwl4zVzRsGRg_Zlwmsn3QGnPXgoGqsGR-8r3920l3OCtIC4crB6uDQZx6-Q85Rtd5E-SYuQ%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | NASA deployed | Powered by AI chips from a company you’ve probably never heard of. NASA trusted it on another planet. The Pentagon uses it. The Department of Energy runs it. And now, even Amazon wants to integrate it into their AI cloud — This same company is still 15 times smaller than Nvidia. Nvidia already soared 7,730%. This one could be next. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. **I wanted to reach out personally and lay out the stakes of New Hampshire’s Senate race.**
National Republicans are determined to flip the Granite State and expand their Senate majority. This is a deeply purple state, and this race is in a _statistical dead heat._
The NRSC has said they’re “all-in” in support of my opponent, John Sununu, and Donald Trump’s allies have said he’s closely watching what happens here. If Trump gets his wish and Senate Republicans gain another seat here, **Democrats could be locked out of a Senate majority for years to come.**

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121738592/590418623/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOWE4NTMzNy1hZmRlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mDhJDiW9kas0k6EFleuaQZM9V2lmNBz64kRwvHb6lAY=&emci=eb833562-49d5-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=09a85337-afde-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=15820338)

Peter,

We're headed out of apple season here in WA-08, and as many of you know, we're famous for our apples.

Kim is a huge apple fan herself. From Honeycrisp to Pink Lady to Washington's own Cosmic Crisp, she'll talk apples with anyone who will listen.

But this season has also been a tough reminder that the current administration is not building an economy that works for our farmers. Before we go any further: [**Will you chip in today to help reelect Kim and flip the House blue in 2026?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121738593/590418624/1807084023?refcode=em25_251221_apples_fndd_gq_act___KSFC_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251221_apples_fndd_gq_act___KSFC_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=09a85337-afde-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOWE4NTMzNy1hZmRlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mDhJDiW9kas0k6EFleuaQZM9V2lmNBz64kRwvHb6lAY=) Our farmers need leaders who will fight for them, _not abandon them to reckless economic policies._

Trump's disastrous tariffs have been hitting our growers from both sides. Production costs are up, making it so farmers need to sell more in order to make a profit. But these tariffs are making exporting apples—a key part of the industry—so costly that buyers are backing out or buying less product than usual. That means more product flooding the U.S. market and prices falling below what many farmers need just to break even.

These policies don't grow our agricultural economy, they don't support our small and family farms, and they certainly don't protect our growers when they face tougher seasons. We need leadership that understands what farmers are up against and will actually have their backs. Kim has been a consistent voice for Washington's farmers, fighting for fair trade, smarter farm policy, and an economy where growers can plan for the future, not just survive the year.

[**It's vital we keep Kim's voice in Congress, and end the Republican agenda that has done nothing but wreak havoc on our farmers. Will you chip in $5 right now to help reelect Kim, flip the House blue, and make way for leadership that will stand with Washington's apple farmers and other vital agricultural groups?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121738594/590418625/1807084023?refcode=em25_251221_apples_fndd_gq_act___KSFC_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251221_apples_fndd_gq_act___KSFC_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=09a85337-afde-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOWE4NTMzNy1hZmRlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mDhJDiW9kas0k6EFleuaQZM9V2lmNBz64kRwvHb6lAY=) clean_body: Peter, We're headed out of apple season here in WA-08, and as many of you know, we're famous for our apples. Kim is a huge apple fan herself. From Honeycrisp to Pink Lady to Washington's own Cosmic Crisp, she'll talk apples with anyone who will listen. But this season has also been a tough reminder that the current administration is not building an economy that works for our farmers. Before we go any further: = Our farmers need leaders who will fight for them, not abandon them to reckless economic policies. Trump's disastrous tariffs have been hitting our growers from both sides. Production costs are up, making it so farmers need to sell more in order to make a profit. But these tariffs are making exporting apples—a key part of the industry—so costly that buyers are backing out or buying less product than usual. That means more product flooding the U.S. market and prices falling below what many farmers need just to break even. These policies don't grow our agricultural economy, they don't support our small and family farms, and they certainly don't protect our growers when they face tougher seasons. We need leadership that understands what farmers are up against and will actually have their backs. Kim has been a consistent voice for Washington's farmers, fighting for fair trade, smarter farm policy, and an economy where growers can plan for the future, not just survive the year. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, Kim Schrier for Congress Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat. = Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress PO Box 2728 Issaquah, WA 98027 United States

10 days... **That's all we have left until our final fundraising deadline of the year.** This is our last grassroots fundraising benchmark to hit – and we need to hit our goal if we’re going to reach the voters we need to take back power in Washington. Right now, Republicans in Congress are more interested in making life more expensive than standing up for working families. _They've had their chance, and they've shown us exactly who they are._ We refuse to let them keep getting away with it. So we’re going to fight like hell to elect Democrats who are unapologetic champions for working people and our basic freedoms. But that fight requires resources. Reaching voters costs money. Running ads costs money. Supporting candidates in competitive races costs money. **The good news is this: If everyone reading this email pitched in $10, we’d hit our goal in no time.** **[So please, chip in whatever you can before our End-of-Year deadline. name: Eric H. Holder, Jr.
subject: I’m thankful for an incredible year of resistance
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
None of this would have been possible without you
|
---

_Today, we're in the midst of a redistricting crisis — and our democracy needs to be actively defended every day. That's why All On The Line is now**Redistricting Action** — a grassroots movement built for the critical fights ahead. Thank you for being part of it._
Peter,
When it comes to attacks on our democracy, 2025 was among the most difficult years we’ve faced as an organization.
Extreme partisan forces passed a racially gerrymandered map in Texas to give themselves an unearned advantage in the midterm elections — a move that set off a ripple effect nationwide. Anti-democracy lawmakers in Missouri then pushed through a redraw that could allow them to steal one more seat. In Utah, partisan actors passed a new gerrymandered map and tried to undermine voter-approved reform.
**But in the midst of these attacks, grassroots supporters like you helped us meet this unprecedented moment.**
And all across America, voters have stood up against gerrymandering. Voters in California resoundingly passed Proposition 50 to cancel out Texas’s gerrymander. Backlash continues to brew in Missouri as voters take their gerrymandered map to the ballot box. And fairness prevailed in Utah when a court struck down the state’s manipulated map.
So much of this would not have been possible without you, Peter. Whether you donated to defend fair maps, signed a petition to protect our democracy, or contacted your member of Congress to speak out in support of fair representation — I am deeply grateful for your unwavering support, however you’ve been able to show up at this moment.
Sincerely,
Eric H. Holder, Jr.
82nd Attorney General of the United States

name: David Hogg
subject: We need a new wave of Watergate babies
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
We’re electing young progressives to repair Trump’s damage.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251221-1&t=1&akid=2969%2E587540%2Eztne_p)
Peter,
Back in 1974, in the aftermath of the Watergate scandal and President Nixon’s resignation, an unprecedented wave of young Democrats swept into Congress. They were called the Watergate Babies — first‐time elected leaders who helped reshape how Washington worked.
The Watergate babies broke the grip of entrenched power structures in the House, challenged the seniority system that protected the status quo, and pushed for transparency and accountability. And they made real progress.
They fought to clean up the corruption that had become too normal (think: the opaque campaign cash, the back‐room deals, hundred-dollar bills in brown paper bags) and helped usher in reforms that gave power back to the people. One of the landmark outcomes of that era was the overhaul of campaign finance law in 1974, putting real limits and enforcement mechanisms in place through the Federal Election Commission so we could finally know who was buying influence in our politics and which special interests were buying out our politicians.
But that fight isn’t finished. And today, we face a new moment of corruption and chaos — from the corruption normalized by Trump and the MAGA movement to lawmakers who let corporate bribes dictate their decisions.
America is due for another wave of Watergate babies who are ready to clean up the mess that Trump and the MAGA movement will inevitably leave behind.
**We need a new generation of young, fierce, unrelenting Democrats** — and that’s exactly what we’re building through our work here at Leaders We Deserve.
**[Peter, will you pitch in $5 now to help us elect the next generation of bold progressives to repair the damage of the Trump years?](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251221_1_watergate.7ydl2Q&refcode=em20251221-2969&amount=5&t=2&akid=2969%2E587540%2Eztne_p)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251221_1_watergate.7ydl2Q&amount=5&refcode=em20251221-2969&t=21&akid=2969%2E587540%2Eztne_p)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20251221-1&t=22&refcode2=2969_587540_ztne_p&akid=2969%2E587540%2Eztne_p)**

name: State Democrats HQ
subject: 2-minute survey: What are three qualities you think are most important for Democrats running for office right now?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
As everyday people face unrelenting attacks from the Trump administration + GOP, we want to know more about what you expect from the Democrats tasked with fighting back.
#
Peter – what are three qualities you think are most important for Democrats running for office right now?
As everyday people face unrelenting attacks from the Trump administration, and the GOP continues to allow more unchecked corruption than ever, we want to know more about what you expect of the Democratic leaders tasked with fighting back during this critical moment.
A real understanding of working families. A willingness to put the American people over powerful special interests. Empathy. Integrity. Anything you think makes a good candidate right now, we want to hear it!
**[So please, will you take 2 minutes today to share three qualities you think are most important for Democrats running for office in 2026?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98821?t=1&akid=9860%2E614464%2EsMIpJT)**
**[SHARE MORE HERE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98821?t=2&akid=9860%2E614464%2EsMIpJT)**

name: Ron Wyden
subject: The GOP's showing how they couldn't care less
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Wyden for Senate
body:
Because of them, health care premiums are going to go through the roof
|
---
| | Last week, **Senate Republicans blocked the passage of a bill that would have extended the ACA tax credits that keep health care costs down for millions of families.** We're now ever closer to the health care cliff, and the fact we're in this situation speaks to the **cost of having Republicans in charge.** When these tax credits expire, health care premiums are going to go through the roof. By letting these tax credits expire, Republicans are sending the message that they couldn't care less if you and your family can afford health care. So while I continue to fight for the ACA credits as the clock ticks down on 2025, I'm gearing up to make sure we **flip Congress blue in 2026. Time to send failed Republican leadership packing.** We need a majority ready on Day One to pass meaningful, lasting health care legislation that stabilizes costs and expands access. [**Will you help me build that majority by donating $3 today?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121739332/590421273/-2044921950?refcode=2025-12-21-aca&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=9fcb4926-b2de-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZmNiNDkyNi1iMmRlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VrPG1BqLCtXv9TeUOy4EUsCz9hKdvc1Lzr3_8xVWoBw=) Thank you, _Ron_ Forwarded Message | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121739333/590421274/2140832878?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTQxMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZmNiNDkyNi1iMmRlLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VrPG1BqLCtXv9TeUOy4EUsCz9hKdvc1Lzr3_8xVWoBw=&emci=553b83aa-afde-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=9fcb4926-b2de-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=9818364)
---
A runaway train is barreling toward our country.
Earlier this year, Republicans voted to raise monthly health insurance premiums for tens of millions of families across the country.
For years, Republicans have been targeting the Affordable Care Act, with seemingly no plan to replace the coverage that millions of families would lose. Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans repeatedly claim that they have _"a plan,"_ but all they have to offer is concepts of a plan.
Earlier this year, I spoke with a couple in Eugene, Oregon, who will see their monthly premiums go from around $400 a month to over $2200 a month. That's a price increase so staggering it leaves you speechless. No one in the United States of America should be expected to pay what amounts to a second mortgage for their health insurance.
Millions of families just like this one will be forced to make tough choices between keeping their health care and covering their rent and groceries.
There's a simple solution to stop this runaway train barreling toward our country: Republicans need to join Democrats and vote for our legislation that will stop this price increase from going into effect on January 1st.
**By refusing to vote for our bill to keep costs down, Republicans are pushing millions of families to the edge of an economic cliff.**
From their trillion-dollar blow to Medicaid to voting to raise premiums, 2025 has been the year Republicans took a sledgehammer to health care in America.
I'm under no illusions that our system is perfect. I think we can all agree that health care costs and insurance companies are spinning out of control, and more needs to be done to rein them in. But the solution is not kicking millions of people off their health care.
That's why I'm working around the clock to keep costs down for families _now_. But then it's time to drastically overhaul the for-profit system that puts profits over patients.
More soon,
_Ron_

clean_body: Because of them, health care premiums are going to go through the roof | | | Last week, Senate Republicans blocked the passage of a bill that would have extended the ACA tax credits that keep health care costs down for millions of families. We're now ever closer to the health care cliff, and the fact we're in this situation speaks to the cost of having Republicans in charge. name: Tobias Read
subject: Add your name: Your voter data belongs to you — not President Trump
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251221em-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251221em-rs]
Hi, it’s Oregon Secretary of State Tobias Read.
I want you to know what’s happening — and why I need you to speak up. Last month, I joined nine other Secretaries of State from across the country to demand transparency from Attorney General Pam Bondi and Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem about how American voter data is being shared and stored.
We set a clear deadline for their response. They missed it.
So today, I’m asking you to join me in demanding transparency, accountability, and answers from President Donald Trump and his lawless administration. Can I count on you to add your name straight away? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Zxp2INlRCkGiRWxomyZ9uA2]
https://secure.ngpvan.com/Zxp2INlRCkGiRWxomyZ9uA2 [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Zxp2INlRCkGiRWxomyZ9uA2]
Their silence matters because earlier this year, the Department of Justice asked my office for deeply personal voter information, including dates of birth, partial Social Security numbers, and full driver’s license numbers.
Without legal authority and without transparency, I refused.
Then we learned that some states’ voter data had been shared with Homeland Security and entered into the SAVE program — a system already known to wrongly flag U.S. citizens as non-citizens.
That puts real people at real risk.
Trust in our elections depends on transparency and accountability at every level of government. When federal officials refuse to answer basic questions about how voter data is being used, that trust is undermined.
That’s why I’m asking you to join me in condemning the silence of Pam Bondi and Kristi Noem and demanding immediate answers. Your voter data belongs to you — not to a federal agency operating behind closed doors. Add your name today and help us send a clear message: when it comes to our elections and our rights, silence is not acceptable. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Zxp2INlRCkGiRWxomyZ9uA2]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Zxp2INlRCkGiRWxomyZ9uA2]
Thank you,
Tobias Read
Secretary of State, Oregon

Portland, OR 97242
United States

| | Peter, I hope you are reading the emails I send, but quite frankly, we're not seeing many of our supporters take action. While Republicans kept a U.S. House seat in our hands on Tuesday in Tennessee, the margin dropped by over HALF from one year ago when President Trump easily won the same district over Kamala Harris. It's got me wondering if there is a correlation between the lull in contributions we're seeing, and the low margins are candidates are winning by this year. Is the writing on the wall, and I'm the only one who doesn't see it? Are you still motivated to win, or have you lost faith in spite of our many accomplishments this past year? I'm curious to know what's going on. Next year's election is going to be a turnout election, and I can assure you that the Left is going to show up. I'm really worried about our folks, voters like you, getting out to vote when President Trump isn't on the ballot. **I 'm asking a small favor:** could you please let me know if you're still with us by making a small contribution of at least $10 today? I know this isn't a poll exactly, but my team and I will use the results of this email to determine the size and the strength of our movement as it stands today. clean_body: I saved YOU the very last spot on the 2025 MAGA Honor Roll | | | | I have ONE spot left on the official 2025 MAGA Honor Roll ... and I’m holding it for YOU. Your name has come across my desk over and over this year — more than once, more than twice — which is exactly why I told my team: “Save the final spot for Fellow American do NOT give it to anyone else.” I’m personally compiling the permanent 2025 MAGA Honor Roll , the exclusive list of my strongest, most loyal patriots from this historic year. This list is going straight to my desk in the Oval Office. I will be reviewing every single name myself tonight. Please don’t make me stay up all night wondering why you weren’t there when I looked for you. Thank you, President Donald J. Trump P.S. When I don’t see your name tonight, I’ll know you let the last spot go to someone else. Don’t do that to me. Confirm now and make my night. | | | | | | | | | | | | Contributions to Trump National Committee JFC Inc. are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. subject: President Trump is worried about you.

body:

| | | | [](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)

Patriot,
This isn't like you at all.
I've been reaching out all year, and **_YOUR NAME_** is still **MISSING **from the 2025 MAGA roll call!
You’re missing out big time.
That’s why I’m reaching out ONE final time in 2025 before my end-of-year deadline! _THIS IS MY FINAL DEADLINE BEFORE THE END OF THE YEAR!_******I’ll be WIDE AWAKE if I don’t see YOUR NAME pop up now!**

[ANSWER TRUMP](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)

name: Raphael Warnock
subject: My friend Roy needs your help
body:
Together, we can lift up a leader who will lift all of us.
| Hi Peter, I’m Reverend Raphael Warnock, United States Senator for the great state of Georgia. I grew up in public housing in Savannah in a family of twelve kids, and I learned early on that when we come together, we can create what Dr. King called the Beloved Community — a world where everyone is respected and cared for. I ran for office because I believe in a Georgia where everyone gets a fair chance: where health care isn’t a privilege, but a right; where our children are safe and respected; where our economy lifts ALL of us — not just those at the top. I know another leader who ran for those same reasons, too. A leader who’s stepping up for our communities, fighting for working families, and refusing to back down when it comes to doing what’s right. **That leader is Roy Cooper.** I’ve watched him work. I’ve seen his commitment. And I can tell you — Roy is the kind of person who will show up for you every day, not just when the cameras are rolling. He’s fighting for affordable health care, for good jobs, for safer communities, and for a democracy that includes every voice. If we want leaders like Roy Cooper in office, we have to stand with them right now. Early support, grassroots support, people-powered support — that’s what makes the difference. **[It takes resources to reach voters, tell the truth, and stand up to the special interests trying to drown out your voice. So I’m asking you to split a donation between my campaign and Roy Cooper to help elect Roy for the U.S. Senate.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3237?t=2&akid=157%2E124072%2EJBhH19)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3238?t=3&akid=157%2E124072%2EJBhH19)
---
[DONATE $10](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3239?t=4&akid=157%2E124072%2EJBhH19)
---
[DONATE $25](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3240?t=5&akid=157%2E124072%2EJBhH19)
---
[DONATE $50](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3241?t=6&akid=157%2E124072%2EJBhH19)
---
[DONATE $100](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3242?t=7&akid=157%2E124072%2EJBhH19)
---
[DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3243?t=8&akid=157%2E124072%2EJBhH19)
---
Together, we’ll build that future. Together, we’ll keep fighting for our children, our communities, and our country. Together, we can lift up a leader who will lift all of us. If you’re able, **[please split a donation between my campaign and Roy Cooper today to get him to the Senate with me.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3237?t=9&akid=157%2E124072%2EJBhH19)** Every single bit helps.
Thank you. May God bless you.
Rev. Raphael Warnock
* * *

The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.

name: Navigating Uncertainty (by Vikram Mansharamani)
subject: South America Turns Right
body:
The region's historical left-leaning politics is turning right just as America is increasing its focus on the Western Hemisphere.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..mJxEfiXRRamgp1NzA_nrRUU2hlDz0FSEMuypX3GFTGI?) for more

# [South America Turns Right](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=182191175&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTkxMTc1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjYzNTQ1NjksImV4cCI6MTc2ODk0NjU2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.78yEC_38XzIrxp23YsXvAZB-8hXI0RQXOAB0YBz0Svk)
### The region's historical left-leaning politics is turning right just as America is increasing its focus on the Western Hemisphere.
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Dec 21| | | ∙| | Preview
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=182191175&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTkxMTc1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NjM1NDU2OSwiZXhwIjoxNzY4OTQ2NTY5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.b7CC5TB6xRqR-I7TxX-k4GDqXIrn-7jbK6sNhtEXjoI&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=182191175&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTkxMTc1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjYzNTQ1NjksImV4cCI6MTc2ODk0NjU2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.78yEC_38XzIrxp23YsXvAZB-8hXI0RQXOAB0YBz0Svk&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=182191175&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTkxMTc1LCJpYXQiOjE3NjYzNTQ1NjksImV4cCI6MTc2ODk0NjU2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.78yEC_38XzIrxp23YsXvAZB-8hXI0RQXOAB0YBz0Svk)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/south-america-turns-right?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a0f7b280-a9b1-4ab3-8b83-61723b5ab6d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Over the course of the past decade, a populist wave has been crashing all over the globe, fueled by inflation, inequality, the cross currents of globalization and technological change, and widespread frustration with elites. A new crop of political leaders - charismatic, bombastic, social media savvy - has capitalized on these grievances, framing elections as increasingly between the common man and the morally bankrupt elites running corrupt institutions that are robbing citizens of opportunity.
These new leaders have reshaped the political landscape in many countries. Here's a quick sampling of rising populist politics around the world:
* **Italy:** [Giorgia Meloni](https://substack.com/redirect/93d7c5d7-2435-49bd-9652-833e5263de98?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has served as Italy's prime minister since 2022. Her campaign focused on national sovereignty, cultural identity, and working-class interests, resonating with voters frustrated by globalization and immigration.
* **Sweden:** The [Sweden Democrats](https://substack.com/redirect/82f9eb7f-db0a-4565-9927-cf52b40f0a92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a right-wing populist party, emerged as the second-most popular party in the country's 2022 elections and has reshaped Sweden's political dialogue. Their platform centers on anti-immigration policies and skepticism toward the European Union.
* **France:** Marine Le Pen's National Rally has steadily gained support and influence over years with its increasing focus on national identity, anti-immigration, and Euroscepticism; a recent [poll](https://substack.com/redirect/7654c574-a68e-475b-9f87-c7b6f1539c1a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) shows its candidate winning the next presidential election.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..oEoaSzQIrPRFpEIILN4H6mGxTfIEEOJlGX3kiYFlfX4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=182191175)
* **Germany:** [Alternative for Germany](https://substack.com/redirect/a652df71-2dd9-41a6-a0cf-3f251b99ff54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (AfD) has become a major force, especially in the east, as immigration has undermined the standing of the country's traditional parties.
* **Indonesia:** [Prabowo Subianto](https://substack.com/redirect/c3f4fbbb-6cfb-4e87-b945-c7e5b506f879?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) won the presidency in 2024, riding a wave of economic discontent and political disillusionment. The former military leader and successful businessman offered to bring discipline to a unruly elite.
* **Pakistan:** Imran Khan capitalized on discontent with the country's traditional hereditary political parties and marshalled the support of Pakistani youth to [become prime minister in 2018](https://substack.com/redirect/5cb618f5-e53c-452c-a61e-0f009af7943d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), only to be deposed in a no-confidence vote that was widely-seen as being engineered by the country's military.

And of course, we can't forget about the political earthquake in America that began with the 2016 election of Donald Trump and the upheaval that followed in its wake. Many of the same themes and issues mentioned above are echoed in America First policies and motivate the MAGA coalition.
It now looks like this populist wave is crashing down on the southern part of our hemisphere as well, as shown by recent election outcomes:
* **Argentenia:** [Javier Milei](https://substack.com/redirect/da2840a6-9edc-4915-8f48-78b1d36a8125?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the leader of La Libertad Avanza, won the Argentinian presidency in 2023 with a strong anti-corruption and populist libertarian economic message.
* **Bolivia:** This past October, [Rodrigo Paz](https://substack.com/redirect/ca689cb9-3ded-40c7-83b1-dedc3bb17023?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ended 20 years of socialist rule when he won the presidential election. Economic discontent was at the forefront of voters' minds when they went to the polls.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..oEoaSzQIrPRFpEIILN4H6mGxTfIEEOJlGX3kiYFlfX4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=182191175)
* **Chile:** Just days ago, [Jose Antonio Kast](https://substack.com/redirect/879814e7-cc91-43ce-ad65-e7f098937216?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was elected president of Chile, handily defeating his leftist opponent. Kast has called for deporting all illegal immigrants, building a border wall, and sending the military into high-crime areas. (Any of this sound familiar?)
* **Brazil:** And don't forget about [Jair Bolsonaro](https://substack.com/redirect/9c38ceeb-3e72-451d-a2bd-d09e2cd5782d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), who foreshadowed this trend when he was elected president in 2018, campaigning on corruption and crime.

And looking forward, [Colombia will head to the polls in 2026.](https://substack.com/redirect/d8d2a248-adc8-4bd6-8458-8f129270a55c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) The country's current socialist president is unable to run, leading to what is expected to be a very contentious election. Will Colombia return to its historical right-leaning politics as voters are increasingly concerned with security and the economy?
* * *
## [US-Colombia Relations Deteriorate](https://substack.com/redirect/63379c84-6614-4123-9768-7d03da0881f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Oct 19
[](https://substack.com/redirect/63379c84-6614-4123-9768-7d03da0881f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[I recently returned from a trip to Colombia where I had the chance to speak with the country's business and political leaders and share my thoughts on global trends, the ongoing trade war, and America's re-emerging interest in their part of the world. In preparation for my speech at the](https://substack.com/redirect/63379c84-6614-4123-9768-7d03da0881f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/63379c84-6614-4123-9768-7d03da0881f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
But what I find most interesting about this South American right turn is that it is coinciding with [America's increasing focus on the region](https://substack.com/redirect/ebd1a31b-d849-4dc7-93f7-fc403b880d2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In what is quickly becoming a 21st century version of the Monroe Doctrine, Trump is in the process of realigning U.S. interests, eschewing far-flung involvements and instead policing our neighborhood and keeping the Chinese and Russians out.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..oEoaSzQIrPRFpEIILN4H6mGxTfIEEOJlGX3kiYFlfX4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=182191175)
While it is natural to assume that ideological birds of a feather would have an affinity for each other, I'd suggest that there is another possible scenario. How will these ...

The region's historical left-leaning politics is turning right just as America is increasing its focus on the Western Hemisphere. | | | Dec 21| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- Over the course of the past decade, a populist wave has been crashing all over the globe, fueled by inflation, inequality, the cross currents of globalization and technological change, and widespread frustration with elites. A new crop of political leaders - charismatic, bombastic, social media savvy - has capitalized on these grievances, framing elections as increasingly between the common man and the morally bankrupt elites running corrupt institutions that are robbing citizens of opportunity. These new leaders have reshaped the political landscape in many countries. Here's a quick sampling of rising populist politics around the world: Italy: has served as Italy's prime minister since 2022. Her campaign focused on national sovereignty, cultural identity, and working-class interests, resonating with voters frustrated by globalization and immigration. Sweden: The , a right-wing populist party, emerged as the second-most popular party in the country's 2022 elections and has reshaped Sweden's political dialogue. Their platform centers on anti-immigration policies and skepticism toward the European Union. France: Marine Le Pen's National Rally has steadily gained support and influence over years with its increasing focus on national identity, anti-immigration, and Euroscepticism; a recent shows its candidate winning the next presidential election. Germany: (AfD) has become a major force, especially in the east, as immigration has undermined the standing of the country's traditional parties. Indonesia: won the presidency in 2024, riding a wave of economic discontent and political disillusionment. The former military leader and successful businessman offered to bring discipline to a unruly elite. Pakistan: Imran Khan capitalized on discontent with the country's traditional hereditary political parties and marshalled the support of Pakistani youth to , only to be deposed in a no-confidence vote that was widely-seen as being engineered by the country's military. And of course, we can't forget about the political earthquake in America that began with the 2016 election of Donald Trump and the upheaval that followed in its wake. Many of the same themes and issues mentioned above are echoed in America First policies and motivate the MAGA coalition. It now looks like this populist wave is crashing down on the southern part of our hemisphere as well, as shown by recent election outcomes: Argentenia: , the leader of La Libertad Avanza, won the Argentinian presidency in 2023 with a strong anti-corruption and populist libertarian economic message. Bolivia: This past October, ended 20 years of socialist rule when he won the presidential election. Economic discontent was at the forefront of voters' minds when they went to the polls. Chile: Just days ago, was elected president of Chile, handily defeating his leftist opponent. Kast has called for deporting all illegal immigrants, building a border wall, and sending the military into high-crime areas. (Any of this sound familiar?) Brazil: And don't forget about , who foreshadowed this trend when he was elected president in 2018, campaigning on corruption and crime. And looking forward, The country's current socialist president is unable to run, leading to what is expected to be a very contentious election. Will Colombia return to its historical right-leaning politics as voters are increasingly concerned with security and the economy? | | | | ---|---|--- | Oct 19 | * But what I find most interesting about this South American right turn is that it is coinciding with . In what is quickly becoming a 21st century version of the Monroe Doctrine, Trump is in the process of realigning U.S. interests, eschewing far-flung involvements and instead policing our neighborhood and keeping the Chinese and Russians out. While it is natural to assume that ideological birds of a feather would have an affinity for each other, I'd suggest that there is another possible scenario. How will these ... name: Jon Ossoff
subject: It really, really matters
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

Friend,
We’re just days away from our critical FEC deadline. If you’re anything like me, you’ve probably gotten a lot of messages like this one asking for your support. And most will say the same thing: _this deadline matters._
But here’s why it _really, really_ matters for this unique Senate race in Georgia, where I am the only Democratic incumbent Senator seeking reelection in a state Trump won:
1\. **WE HAVE TO SHOW STRENGTH.** There are three Republican candidates running against me, and national Republicans have already spent millions of dollars on TV and digital ads attacking me. A strong showing proves to my opponents (and their Super PAC allies) — that our movement in Georgia is thriving and unstoppable.

2\. **THERE’S NO TIME TO LOSE.** To win decisively, my team is already building a huge statewide campaign. That means more rallies. More organizers. More outreach — and early outreach — to voters in every corner of the state. That all takes resources right now. We’re building and we’ve got to keep building bigger and faster every day.

[**So please, if you can, use this link to contribute anything you can to Mark Kelly and my reelection campaign in Georgia before our FEC deadline in 10 days. I’d be grateful to have your support.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-ossoff.o6kuHF&refcode=em251221-2-90&t=1&akid=6307%2E6688874%2ER_lVhn)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1396?c0=mek-em-ossoff.Xq-TZr&refcode=em251221-2-90&t=20&akid=6307%2E6688874%2ER_lVhn)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1397?c0=mek-em-ossoff.n-TdNG&refcode=em251221-2-90&t=21&akid=6307%2E6688874%2ER_lVhn)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1390?c0=mek-em-ossoff.U96K3E&refcode=em251221-2-90&t=22&akid=6307%2E6688874%2ER_lVhn)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1391?c0=mek-em-ossoff.OprxVC&refcode=em251221-2-90&t=23&akid=6307%2E6688874%2ER_lVhn)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1392?c0=mek-em-ossoff.BYnxB7&refcode=em251221-2-90&t=24&akid=6307%2E6688874%2ER_lVhn)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1395?c0=mek-em-ossoff.OpjaJE&refcode=em251221-2-90&t=25&akid=6307%2E6688874%2ER_lVhn)**
---
****
I don’t love asking for money. I don’t think _anyone_ does.
But the reality is I have to. I can’t win without you.
_Citizens United_ opened the dam for limitless dark money to flood into elections.
_That’s a big part of why Congress is broken and corrupted. It’s why we have really expensive elections._
And also it’s why campaigns like mine have to build serious grassroots fundraising operations from Day One.
I don’t love asking for money. I don’t think anyone does.
_I need your help, friend!_
**I am literally MAGA’s #1 target.**
They will spare no expense and try every dirty trick.
Yes, this election will be tough.
But I know how to win tough fights. I’ve done it before.
Let’s do it again.

clean_body: Friend, We’re just days away from our critical FEC deadline. If you’re anything like me, you’ve probably gotten a lot of messages like this one asking for your support. And most will say the same thing: this deadline matters. But here’s why it really, really matters for this unique Senate race in Georgia, where I am the only Democratic incumbent Senator seeking reelection in a state Trump won: 1\. WE HAVE TO SHOW STRENGTH. There are three Republican candidates running against me, and national Republicans have already spent millions of dollars on TV and digital ads attacking me. A strong showing proves to my opponents (and their Super PAC allies) — that our movement in Georgia is thriving and unstoppable. 2\. THERE’S NO TIME TO LOSE. To win decisively, my team is already building a huge statewide campaign. That means more rallies. More organizers. More outreach — and early outreach — to voters in every corner of the state. That all takes resources right now. We’re building and we’ve got to keep building bigger and faster every day. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | I don’t love asking for money. I don’t think anyone does. But the reality is I have to. I can’t win without you. Citizens United opened the dam for limitless dark money to flood into elections. That’s a big part of why Congress is broken and corrupted. It’s why we have really expensive elections. And also it’s why campaigns like mine have to build serious grassroots fundraising operations from Day One. I don’t love asking for money. I don’t think anyone does. I need your help, friend! I am literally MAGA’s #1 target. They will spare no expense and try every dirty trick. Yes, this election will be tough. But I know how to win tough fights. I’ve done it before. Let’s do it again. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | | | | | | | | ** Thank you. Jon Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate

This ain't the same Party that it was when I first entered the big leagues. It's become a paradise for Liberal hogwash and downright anti-American behavior.
Whether it's...

* Rioting in cities across America and burning Old Glory
* Storming ICE facilities, doxxing agents, and throwing sandwiches at law enforcement
* Having actual COMMUNISTS run some of our greatest cities

It sure seems like once you think the Democrats can't stoop any lower, they grab a shovel and prove you wrong... Guess they never heard the first rule of holes: if you're in one, STOP DIGGING.
_But let me tell you the God-honest truth. They have the money to do it and if we don 't get our act together, they just might put us in their place._
Right now, the Democrats have a well-oiled fundraising machine that is raking in the big bucks from its out-of-touch, elitist donors - 100 million bucks, to be exact.
After seeing that, I knew I had to step in.
**_[So my good friend Majority Leader Steve Scalise and I got to talking. body:

Peter —
A lot of Democratic leaders have been afraid to stand up to Elon Musk and billionaires like him.
They fear the massive sums of money that will be spent against them if they tell the truth about what’s really broken in America.
Not James. James told Elon Musk what he really thinks on his own social media platform:
[](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/1093?t=1&akid=336%2E1347274%2EhH6CuP)

We need leaders willing to speak truth to power. We deserve leaders who will fight for us.
This is who James is. Always has been.
\- Team Talarico

body:

2026 is the firewall.
If we lose the House or fail to take back the Senate, the left finishes the job they started.
That’s why Turning Point PAC is deploying the most sophisticated ballot-chasing operation the conservative movement has ever built — bigger and more advanced than anything we've done before.
**In Arizona, Georgia, Nevada, Pennsylvania, Wisconsin, and Michigan we are:**

* **Identifying** every single low-propensity America First voter

* **Registering** unregistered Patriots

* **Chasing** every legal ballot with thousands of paid chasers using our first-of-its-kind mobile app

* **Curing** ballots and building authentic relationships door by door, call by call, text by text

* **Constructing** a permanent Red Wall that no Democrat can breach in 2026, 2028, or beyond

The left had a decade head start and billions from their billionaire donors. We're not just catching up — _we're surpassing them in the states that decide everything, right now._
**[Whether it’s $1 or $100 — every dollar you give today fuels paid ballot chasers in the field, banking the votes that secure Senate seats, House majorities, and the America First agenda.](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e5665705/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYW5lZG90LmNvbS90cHBhYy9zYl9lbS0zX2Rvbl8yMDI1MTIyMS8/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNTEyMjE/LbmNsZ2t3dW95dg/qP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjIxNTdfZnRkMQ/gaUh6XQ/s8qbd27df93)**
Help us build the unbreakable machine and chart a path to victory.

Turning Point PAC Team

Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.’s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes.

Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee’s name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information. Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law. Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.’s non-contribution account are not tax-deductible for federal income tax purposes. Paid for by Turning Point PAC, Inc. | Peter,
---
| Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud.

He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus.

He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness.

And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible.

I saw it up close -- _Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. _

But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything.

_**He started observing the Sabbath. **_

Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance.

_But as an act of obedience. _

As a way of saying, **_" God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." _**

Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped.

He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield.

No email. No media. No work.

He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children.

It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much.

But over time, it changed him.

And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned...

| Peter, like I mentioned, this isn't a political book.

It's not a culture war manifesto.

It's a call to stop.

To rest. To breathe. To return to God.

**Because Charlie believed America hadn 't just forgotten truth... It had forgotten trust. **

Trust that the world keeps spinning when you step away.

Trust that God is in control.

Trust that rest is not weakness -- it's worship.

**In _Stop, in the Name of God,_ you'll discover: **

🔥 How Charlie reclaimed the Sabbath in a world built on burnout

🔥 What sacred rest gave back to him -- as a father, husband, and believer

🔥 Why he believed this message could spark revival in a restless generation

Charlie wrote this book because he knew what it meant to nearly lose his peace -- and what it felt like to find it again.

That's the heartbeat of his final message.

He left behind a family. He left behind a movement. And he left behind this truth...

For anyone ready to step off the treadmill... and walk back toward what's eternal.

Peter, thank you -- in advance -- for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage.

These are the very things Charlie gave his life for.

May the message he left behind lead you -- just as it led him -- to a deeper trust in God...

A stronger love for your family...

And a renewed peace in your own soul.

| **For Charlie, **

**Justin Streiff **

clean_body: he wrote this... Charlie’s final message—support Turning Point USA & receive his last book. | | | Peter, | Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud. He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus. He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness. And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible. I saw it up close -- Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything. He started observing the Sabbath. Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance. But as an act of obedience. As a way of saying, " God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped. He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield. No email. No media. No work. He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children. It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much. But over time, it changed him. And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned... | | This was Charlie's final message. His final act of trust. A book not about politics... but about peace. It 's called: Stop, in the Name of God. | | Peter, like I mentioned, this isn't a political book. It's not a culture war manifesto. It's a call to stop. To rest. To breathe. To return to God. Because Charlie believed America hadn 't just forgotten truth... It had forgotten trust. Trust that the world keeps spinning when you step away. Trust that God is in control. Trust that rest is not weakness -- it's worship. In Stop, in the Name of God, you'll discover: 🔥 How Charlie reclaimed the Sabbath in a world built on burnout 🔥 What sacred rest gave back to him -- as a father, husband, and believer 🔥 Why he believed this message could spark revival in a restless generation Charlie wrote this book because he knew what it meant to nearly lose his peace -- and what it felt like to find it again. That's the heartbeat of his final message. | | Charlie Kirk didn't waste his time on earth. He left behind a family. He left behind a movement. And he left behind this truth... For anyone ready to step off the treadmill... and walk back toward what's eternal. Peter, thank you -- in advance -- for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage. These are the very things Charlie gave his life for. May the message he left behind lead you -- just as it led him -- to a deeper trust in God... A stronger love for your family... And a renewed peace in your own soul. | For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating Officer Turning Point USA | | | P.S.Stop, in the Name of God is Charlie Kirk's final message -- written in obedience, shaped by Scripture, and grounded in trust in the Lord. | | 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044

This week's **Blue Amp Media Video RoundUp** brings together a set of conversations that cut through the noise and get to the stakes--political, moral, and human--driving the headlines right now.
From investigative reporting on U.S. military actions and erased evidence, to hard conversations about democratic breakdown and the long-term cost of normalized extremism, these shows share a common thread: accountability. Not the performative kind, but the uncomfortable, necessary kind that power would rather avoid.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.w62bMxGyHwditvs8tUXhPzrKcWqlDYa6_pucSd5Q27E?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=182272608)
You'll hear directly from journalists, veterans, strategists, and organizers who aren't interested in hot takes or cable-news theater. Instead, they're asking the questions that actually matter: Who is making these decisions? Who pays the price? And why do the same patterns keep repeating--until someone forces them into the light?
If you missed any of these conversations, this roundup is your chance to catch up. If you saw them already, they're worth revisiting--because none of this is theoretical anymore.

name: Mike Johnson, 56th Speaker of the House
subject: Big News
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Peter, I'm not alone in believing you truly deserve this. My colleagues and I at House GOP Headquarters were all in agreement.

clean_body: Peter, I'm not alone in believing you truly deserve this. My colleagues and I at House GOP Headquarters were all in agreement. In fact, we've made a unanimous decision. If there's anyone who has a record worthy of this, it's you. First of all, Congratulations! This is reserved only for top supporters of our movement. But I have to say, you've been so outstanding in your record of support that you've built up a profile that falls within the TOP Tier of GOP Patriots. one of House GOP's most trusted patriots, and you deserve to have the status to reflect that! And before you click, I want to let you know that we'll be leaning on you for your opinion on matters as we move forward. I'm putting this deal on the table because we can't afford to lose our most trusted allies when our majority is as thin as it is right now! You, God Bless You and God Bless America! Sincerely, Mike Johnson56th Speaker of the House On behalf of House GOP HQ (Platinum Committee)| | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Deb Haaland
subject: let me explain
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
Getting here wasn't easy, friend

| | | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/1102?refcode=jtk102-fr-nd&t=1&akid=6356%2E400544%2EmnNb2n)
---
Friend: In a moment, **[I'm going to ask you to make a $5 donation to my campaign.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2930?refcode=jtk102-fr-nd&t=2&akid=6356%2E400544%2EmnNb2n)** But first, will you let me explain who I am, why I'm in this race, and the difference your donation will make?
As the daughter of a Pueblo woman, I was taught the value of hard work from a very young age.

My mother was born and raised in a train boxcar, where the Laguna people built a community far from their home. She proudly served in the U.S. Navy, raised four kids as a military wife, and devoted more than two decades to Indian education at the Bureau of Indian Affairs.

I am blessed to come from generations of resilient and fierce Pueblo women – **and I am the person, mother, and leader I am today because of them.**
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2931?refcode=jtk102-fr-nd&t=3&akid=6356%2E400544%2EmnNb2n)
**But getting here wasn't easy, friend, and the hard work and resilience I learned proved essential.
**
Like many New Mexicans, I know what it’s like to struggle. I was a single mom living paycheck to paycheck, relying on government programs like SNAP, WIC, and Medicaid to help keep my child fed and healthy. **Every day, as hard as it was, I had to get back up and keep going.
**
But my struggle made me fierce. It fortified my resolve to build a brighter future – not only for my child, _but also for every family dreaming of and fighting for a better life._

That's why I decided to run for office in 2018. _And I won._ I became one of the first Native American women elected to Congress, and in 2021, the first Native American cabinet secretary in U.S. history.
**
Now, I'm running to be the next governor of New Mexico to help move our state toward a future where everyone can thrive.**
**Every step of my journey has been possible because people like you believed in me,****friend****. So please:**[**Can I count on you to chip in $5 or more right now to help me win, keep New Mexico blue, and deliver progress for working families?**](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2930?refcode=jtk102-fr-nd&t=4&akid=6356%2E400544%2EmnNb2n)
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $10](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2933?refcode=jtk102-fr-nd&t=5&akid=6356%2E400544%2EmnNb2n)
---
| | [Donate $35](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/2934?refcode=jtk102-fr-nd&t=6&akid=6356%2E400544%2EmnNb2n)
---

clean_body: Getting here wasn't easy, friend | | | Friend: In a moment, But first, will you let me explain who I am, why I'm in this race, and the difference your donation will make? As the daughter of a Pueblo woman, I was taught the value of hard work from a very young age. My mother was born and raised in a train boxcar, where the Laguna people built a community far from their home. She proudly served in the U.S. Navy, raised four kids as a military wife, and devoted more than two decades to Indian education at the Bureau of Indian Affairs. I am blessed to come from generations of resilient and fierce Pueblo women – and I am the person, mother, and leader I am today because of them. But getting here wasn't easy, friend, and the hard work and resilience I learned proved essential. Like many New Mexicans, I know what it’s like to struggle. I was a single mom living paycheck to paycheck, relying on government programs like SNAP, WIC, and Medicaid to help keep my child fed and healthy. Every day, as hard as it was, I had to get back up and keep going. But my struggle made me fierce. It fortified my resolve to build a brighter future – not only for my child, but also for every family dreaming of and fighting for a better life. That's why I decided to run for office in 2018. And I won. I became one of the first Native American women elected to Congress, and in 2021, the first Native American cabinet secretary in U.S. history. Now, I'm running to be the next governor of New Mexico to help move our state toward a future where everyone can thrive. Every step of my journey has been possible because people like you believed in me,friend. So please: If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | I know that it takes a village – in life, and in politics – and that's why I'll never stop fighting for our communities. Thank you for standing with me, Deb | | | | |
